Submit proposals to funding agencies, such as AIC,
SSG, National Silver Academy (NSA) and National Council of Service Services (NCSS). Evaluate and monitor the effectiveness of DSA training programmes and consultancy services to ensure the quality. Work with DSG Communications team to raise awareness of Academy training programmes and consultancy services, and
publicise training courses and events. Conduct training in own area of expertise as a dementia practitioner, where relevant. Any other duties as assigned. Requirements Degree in Business, Marketing or Management. At least 5 years of relevant work experience. Competent in networking and multi-agency collaborations. Experience or familiarity with SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) frameworks, including use of the Training Partner Gateway (TPG). Possess strong administrative and project management skills. Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). Previous experience in eldercare, healthcare or adult education sector is advantageous. Certification in training/education/courseware development will be an added advantage.

title: Deputy General Manager, Regional Compliance (ASEAN)
description: Overview We are seeking a senior compliance leader to design and enforce a unified governance framework across our ASEAN subsidiaries. Reporting to the Regional Head of Legal & Compliance, you will act as a strategic partner to the business, ensuring that our cross-border network expansion (IPLC, IP Transit, SD-WAN) and multi-country operations align strictly with the diverse legal, tax, and telecom licensing requirements of the region. Key Responsibilities: Regional Regulatory Strategy: Oversee the regulatory roadmap for entering and operating in ASEAN markets (including Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, and Philippines). Manage the acquisition and maintenance of necessary telecom licenses (e.g., NFP/NSP/NTC) and serve as the primary escalation point for regional regulators. Cross-Border Operational Governance: Harmonize "General Affairs" and HR compliance across subsidiaries. Ensure local offices adhere to their respective labor laws (e.g., Employment Act, Omnibus Law) regarding hiring, expatriate visas, and statutory benefits. Data Privacy & Sovereignty: Lead the regional data privacy strategy. Navigate complex data sovereignty laws to ensure the compliant transfer of customer data and internal records between ASEAN entities and Regional HQ. Commercial Risk Management: Sit on the Joint Governance committee to review complex multi-country connectivity deals. Audit vendor and partner contracts to ensure "back-to-back" liability protection and adherence to anti-corruption (ABC) standards. Internal Audit & Control: Conduct regular compliance audits of local ASEAN offices, focusing on procurement integrity, safety (WSH/EHS) standards, and financial reporting. Stakeholder Management: Translate regional strategy into local compliance roadmaps and act as the bridge between local ASEAN teams and the Group Headquarters. Job RequirementsExperience & Qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree in Law, International Business, or a related discipline. A Master’s degree or professional compliance certification is an advantage. Minimum 12 years of experience in Compliance, Legal, or Regional Operations, with at least 5 years in a leadership role within the Telecommunications or ICT industry. Proven track record of managing compliance or operations across multiple ASEAN jurisdictions (experience with Indonesia, Vietnam, or Malaysia is highly valued). Technical Skills & Knowledge: Strong understanding of cross-border telecom regulations (IPLC, VPN, Internet) and the licensing frameworks within Southeast Asia. Familiarity with regional data privacy laws (PDPA, GDPR principles) and data sovereignty requirements. Knowledge of regional labor regulations and "General Affairs" management. Other Requirements: Business-level proficiency in English and Mandarin is required. This role necessitates frequent communication with Product and Engineering teams at our Regional Headquarters in China, as well as the review of technical contracts and legal policies in Mandarin. Willingness to travel within the ASEAN region (approx. 30-40%) as business needs require. Legally eligible to work in Singapore.

title: Manager / Senior Manager, Policy Office (SPD)
description: SSG is a dynamic and forward-thinking organization dedicated to empowering individuals and shaping the future of Singapore's workforce. As a statutory board under the Ministry of Education, SSG leads the charge in driving the SkillsFuture movement, a national initiative that promotes lifelong learning and skills development. With a strong focus on innovation and collaboration, SSG works closely with employers, training providers, and individuals to create a vibrant ecosystem of learning and growth. By offering a wide range of initiatives, programs, and funding schemes, SSG enables individuals to unlock their full potential, acquire new competencies, and stay ahead in a rapidly changing job market. Join the SPD team in SSG. We drive strategic coherence across the organisation, while sparking new forms of thinking and conceptualise new national and agency level policies to advance SSG’s mission in supporting the SkillsFuture movement. We also advance research aligned to the SSG strategic priorities to facilitate evidence-based policymaking, programme development and improvements to practice. The Manager/Senior Manager in the Policy Office within the Strategic Planning Division is an individual contributor role responsible for driving high-impact SkillsFuture projects, coordinating whole-of-government policies, advancing international strategies, and conducting horizon scans on emerging trends. This role is essential in supporting Singapore's workforce skills development efforts and ensuring effective policy reviews. The position demands a strategic thinker who can synthesize information, engage stakeholders, and adapt to changing priorities. Project Management & Implementation · Conceptualize, coordinate, and implement high-impact SkillsFuture projects. · Define project scope, implementation parameters, and desired outcomes. · Track progress and evaluate results to ensure project success. Policy Planning & Coordination : · Plan and coordinate whole-of-government policies to advance the Jobs and Skills agenda. · Conduct horizon scans on emerging trends in the CET landscape to support policy reviews. · Perform secretariat duties for key meeting platforms. International Strategy & Collaboration : · Advance SSG's international strategy by collaborating with overseas counterparts. · Benchmark Singapore's progress and learn best practices from international engagements. Communication & Stakeholder Engagement : · Synthesize information with attention to detail and substantiate findings using written and visual formats. · Communicate clearly in both written and verbal forms. · Engage various stakeholders and drive fruitful collaborations. Adaptability & Resourcefulness : · Multi-task effectively, balancing competing priorities under tight deadlines and ambiguity. · Be resourceful and demonstrate a strong drive to get things done. · Anticipate needs and make judgment calls within your sphere of responsibilities. · Work agilely to support new projects or emerging needs. Requirements: · The required competencies to execute the job duties proficiently · Manager: Up to 6 years of relevant work experience in policy planning. Those with no relevant experience but have been involved in relevant projects or short stints in similar areas / functions can apply · Senior Manager: At least 6 years of relevant work experience in policy planning · Strong quantitative skills and experience in policy and planning work · Ability to synthesize broad information with attention to detail · Effective communication skills in both written and verbal formats · Proven ability to engage stakeholders and drive collaborations · Resourcefulness and adaptability in managing changing priorities Successful candidates will be offered a 1-year contract with potential for a 1-year extension and consideration for permanent tenure thereafter. Candidates are encouraged to sign up for a Careers & Skills Passport (CSP) account and include your CSP public profile in your resume.  Please check out www.myskillsfuture.gov.sg for details on the CSP.

title: IT EXECUTIVE
description: We are seeking for an IT Executive to support the effective operation of the school’s technology infrastructure and systems. This role assists in managing network resources, providing technical support, maintaining software applications, and ensuring adherence to IT policies and data protection standards. The IT Executive works closely with the Head of IT to deliver reliable, secure, and user-friendly IT services to the school community. Key Responsibilities 1. Network and Systems Support Assist in the maintenance and monitoring of the school’s network infrastructure, including internet connectivity, Wi-Fi, and internal systems. Support the implementation of security protocols and ensure safe use of IT resources. Troubleshoot network and system issues to ensure stable and reliable operations. 2. IT Applications and System Administration Provide support in deploying, updating, and maintaining educational and administrative applications such as Seesaw, iSAMS, SchoolsBuddy. Assist in ensuring effective integration between different software applications. Support updates and content management for the school’s website and online learning platforms. 3. Technical Support and Customer Service Provide timely technical support to staff, students, and parents across hardware, software, and connectivity issues. Manage and update the IT helpdesk system to ensure efficient issue tracking and resolution. Assist in preparing and delivering basic IT training sessions and user guides to improve digital literacy. 4. Hardware and Asset Management Support the setup, configuration, and maintenance of IT equipment including computers, tablets, printers, projectors, and classroom technologies. Maintain accurate records of IT assets and assist in lifecycle management (deployment, replacement, disposal). 5. Data Management and Compliance Assist in data backup routines, storage management, and recovery processes. Ensure compliance with data protection regulations and uphold confidentiality of school data. 6. Vendor and Stakeholder Coordination Assist the Head of IT in liaising with IT vendors and service providers for maintenance, repairs, and procurement. Coordinate with staff and external partners to support smooth delivery of IT services. Qualifications Education and Experience Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. 1–3 years of experience in IT support or system administration. Experience in an educational or international school institution is an advantage. Technical Skills Basic knowledge of network systems, cybersecurity principles, and IT infrastructure. Experience with Windows, macOS, and mobile device management. Familiarity with cloud solutions and common educational technologies (Seesaw, iSAMS, SchoolsBuddy, etc.) Strong troubleshooting skills in hardware, software, and connectivity issues. Soft Skills Strong customer service orientation and problem-solving abilities. Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to support users with varying levels of digital proficiency. Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team. Organised, proactive, and attentive to detail. Safeguarding: The Perse School (Singapore) is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people and expects all staff and volunteers to share this commitment. All candidates will be subject to rigorous safeguarding checks, including reference checks, an enhanced DBS (Disclosure and Barring Service) check, and verification of qualifications and employment history. As part of our commitment to safeguarding, all staff are required to uphold the school’s safeguarding policies, attend regular training, and promote a safe and inclusive environment for all students. Any gaps in employment must be explained, and candidates should be prepared to discuss their approach to safeguarding in education as part of the recruitment process. when rating patches Stakeholder Engagement: Present vulnerability scanning results, security testing results, and security incidents to management Conduct security awareness training for users Act as bridge between technical teams and management What We Are Looking For Experience & Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, Cybersecurity, or related field Minimum 3 years relevant experience Experience in network security, secure application development, cryptography, mobile security, cloud hosting, or DevSecOps Experience working with teams to create security policies and platforms (AWS Security Hub, Microsoft Defender/Sentinel for Cloud) Understand cybersecurity investigation processes and vulnerability assessment/penetration testing (VAPT) Personal Qualities: Good title: Compliance Officer (Regulatory & Licensing)

You will be at the forefront of a global compliance team, conducting independent regulatory licensing research; diving into new areas, regions or jurisdictions; and collaborating with the internal team to develop solutions for obtaining and maintaining licenses in different jurisdictions. title: Business Analyst (Data Policy)
description: About the Role We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst (Data Policy) to support a Singapore Government Agency in developing, implementing, and maintaining robust data governance and data protection policies. This role involves policy analysis, development of data governance frameworks, regulatory compliance, and cross-agency stakeholder collaboration. You will help drive responsible data usage and ensure alignment with national standards, public sector regulations, and organisational objectives. Key Responsibilities 1. Data Policy & Standards Development Develop, review, and refine data governance policies, standards, frameworks, and guidelines. Translate Whole-of-Government (WOG) data policies into practical processes for internal adoption. Support policy areas including data access governance, data sharing, consent management, and third-party data management. 2. Governance Advisory & Compliance Provide advisory to internal teams on data protection legislation (e.g., PDPA) and public sector data governance requirements. Assess data governance implications for new programmes, digital initiatives, and data-sharing arrangements. Review agreements, contracts, and MOUs to ensure alignment with confidentiality and data protection obligations. 3. Stakeholder Engagement Partner with internal departments, government agencies, and external partners on data governance and policy matters. Serve as the primary liaison for data policy queries, submissions, and consultations involving WOG stakeholders. Facilitate discussions and align expectations with stakeholders across multiple functional areas. 4. Capability Building & Awareness Develop training materials and conduct engagement sessions to enhance data governance awareness across the organisation. Promote best practices in data management and support adoption of enterprise-wide data governance frameworks. 5. Operational Governance Support Manage, track, and report on data access, data-sharing requests, and related governance workflows. Support continuous improvement of data governance processes and compliance mechanisms. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Management, Engineering, Business Analytics, Law, or a related field. Experience Minimum 7 years of experience in one or more of the following areas: Data governance, dat protection, or data management Formulation of data policies, standards, or frameworks Information lifecycle management or records management Conducting risk assessments or business audits Reviewing data protection and confidentiality clauses in agreements Key Skills Strong analytical and critical-thinking abilities; able to synthesize complex information. High attention to detail with the ability to understand broader policy and organisational implications. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain policy and technical governance concepts clearly. Strong stakeholder management, collaboration, and negotiation skills. Comfortable working in a dynamic and fast-paced environment. Programme or project management skills are an advantage. Preferred Skills Professional certifications in data governance, data protection, privacy, analytics, or related areas. Experience with Singapore Government IM8 policies. Experience in data or cybersecurity.

title: Senior Audit Manager - T&O
description: JOB SUMMARY •    Group Internal Audit & Investigations (GIAI) in Standard Chartered Bank is looking for an exceptional and dynamic Senior Audit Manager to join our Financial Crime Surveillance (FCS) Operations team within the Technology & Operations portfolio. •    The role holder is expected to manage, lead and deliver the audit work covering key risks of FCS Operations and should have hands-on experience in auditing FCS Operations. •    Possess a deep understanding on digital assets, including but not limited to trading, processing and transaction monitoring of digital assets. •    Strong communicator, both written and verbal, with ability to clearly explain issues to Management and write audit issues, reports, and opinions. •    Ability to manage the team, provide coaching and mentoring to team members and lead auditors of a wide range of skills to deliver audits. •    Strong analytical skills with hands-on experience on Python, Power BI, Tableau, SQL. •    Proven skills in prompt engineering queries for artificial intelligence (AI), in the aid of audit planning, execution and delivery. RESPONSIBILITIES Audit Delivery •    Act as Team Manager or Team Leader to deliver audits timely. •    Execute audit work and issue validation in an efficient and effective manner, within the given budget and timelines. •    Provide clear guidance, detailed review, and supervision of the audit team’s work, so that audit deliverables meet quality standards. •    Clearly identify the risks and impact of issues during issue writing, agreeing these issues with management and obtaining quality management action plans to mitigate the risk. •    Provide guidance to the team on business/audit technical knowledge and management skills. •    Champion innovation and increase the use of leading edge methods, such as artificial intelligence and data analytics, in audit assignments. •    Ensure timely and open communication throughout the audit process, within GIAI and with the auditee. •    Track the implementation/delivery of the agreed issues and action plans for the audits assigned, understanding the key risks arising, provide advice on resolution of issues to auditees/action plan owners and escalate audit findings that remain unresolved. •    Carry out their role in line with the Audit Charter and remain independent from management and free from interference. Personal Behaviours •    Model valued behaviours and foster an environment that recognises positive contributions. •    Engage with peers to enhance knowledge sharing. •    Seek external perspectives to improve audit work. •    Provide expertise to support GIAI audit teams. •    Implement and support department initiatives, including planning and training. People & Talent •    Build and maintain relationships with key GIAI and FCS Operations stakeholders to understand business developments including regulatory concerns, identify emerging risks, and facilitate execution of audit work. •    Share knowledge, skills and experience with team members and the wider GIAI and provide advisory to internal auditors from both Business and Technology teams. •    Contribute to building a high performing collaborative function through regular feedback and coaching. Risk Management •    Complete relevant risk assessment activities in a timely and effective manner, including the completion of the Risk Assessment template. •    Assist in continuous auditing and monitoring of key risks in the portfolio. •    Follow changes in the laws and regulations to identify emerging risks. •    Utilise the risk assessment process and stakeholder engagement to determine an anticipatory audit plan. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Designated FCS Operations stakeholders, typically related to individual audit assignments and the assigned portfolio. •    GIAI stakeholders, including Team Members, Team Leaders, Team Managers, Accountable Executive Team, Portfolio, Functional, Country and Cluster Heads of Audit. Other Responsibilities •    Embed Here for Good and Group’s brand and values in GIAI. •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. Our Ideal Candidate •    Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or a related field •    10+ years of hands-on experience in the Audit, and/or Banking and Financial Services industry •    Chartered Accountant, Certified Internal Audit (CIA), Digital Assets related certification, Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist (CAMS) will be advantageous. Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Internal Audit •    Data Analytics (Python, Power BI, Tableau, SQL) •    Digital Assets •    Microsoft Word/Excel/PowerPoint About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. A Manager-level role at Bestar involves overseeing a portfolio of clients (ranging from SMEs to international subsidiaries) and leading a team of associates. description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. title: Executive / Assistant Manager / Manager, SNEF Lab, Policy Division
description: Job Description & Requirements The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is a trade union of employers. SNEF represents employers in Singapore’s unique brand of tripartism, where employers, unions and workers, and the Government work together to come up with win-win solutions to national challenges. This has been key to Singapore’s economic growth. SNEF is strengthening its policy and research capabilities to enhance advocacy for employers’ interests. As part of the SNEF Lab team, you must have an interest in labour policies to support SNEF’s research and sense-making work to drive SNEF’s efforts in developing and promoting progressive workplace practices, enhancing labour market flexibility and advancing tripartism. Your responsibilities include: · Monitor and analyse global and local trends, including socioeconomic data of labour markets, to anticipate or identify emerging HR-related challenges and opportunities for employers in Singapore – to bring them to the forefront of tripartite discussions before they evolve into significant challenges for employers in Singapore · Develop research approaches to collect and analyse data to design and shape employer-centric labour policy solutions to (a) address employers’ challenges or meet employers’ needs and (b) respond to proposals from the Government or unions · Execute these research approaches, including in partnership with academics, institutions of higher learning, tripartite partners and government agencies on areas of common interest · Improve SNEF’s research capabilities, including data collection and stakeholder engagement · Plan and execute research projects in areas of salary benchmarking, benefits review, employee engagement and other talent management programmes. · Support content and delivery of workshops, events and seminars. The position will also have opportunities to work with the rest of the Policy Division to develop and shape SNEF’s positions on various workplace and workforce issues to effectively and proactively shape manpower policies in Singapore. This includes developing evidence-based policies or recommendations to further the interests of responsible employers and sustainable businesses, engaging the employer community to better understand their immediate and longer-term needs and concerns, and evaluating and devising robust and balanced responses to proposals from the Government or unions. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Social Sciences (political science, sociology, organisation psychology, etc) HR, Statistics or other related disciplines. Fresh graduates are welcomed. An interest in international labour and experience in planning regional/ international events is a plus. You must: · Possess good written, presentation and verbal communication skills · Be proficient in applications such as PowerPoint, Word, Excel and Canva · Be experienced with data analytics/programming applications such as Tableau/PowerBI, Stata, R or SPSS · Be versatile, resourceful and strong in stakeholders management · Be a team player, have a strategic and growth mindset and a keen eye to details If hired, your designation will be commensurate with your work scope and experience. By submitting your resume, you have given SNEF consent to collect data which will be used for the assessment and evaluation of your suitability for employment within the organisation.

title: Regional Market Manager
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition CIMC RAFFLES OFFSHORE (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD. Job Summary You will develop regional market customers and establish the sales network layout for the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and international markets, driving strategic growth and fostering long-term business relationships in the offshore engineering and shipbuilding sectors. Responsibilities Develop and implement strategic sales network layouts tailored to the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and international markets by conducting in-depth research on regional needs and cultural contexts. Collect and analyze market industry data, including laws, regulations, tax policies, and environmental factors, to identify market trends, target customers, competitor activities, and optimize pricing strategies. Gather and evaluate customer information through diverse channels such as internet research, emails, exhibitions, site visits, and direct communication to identify and pursue potential cooperation opportunities. Build and maintain strong relationships with regional market customers by organizing and participating in visits and receptions, presenting company capabilities and products, understanding customer needs, and delivering customized service plans. Assist in qualifying potential customers by reviewing compliance with local regulations to ensure eligibility for project bidding. Support the formulation of project bidding strategies by reviewing bidding documents for alignment with customer requirements and local regulations, and contribute to determining final bid pricing. Participate in contract negotiations to ensure business terms comply with local laws and market regulations while safeguarding company interests. Review contract terms to assess execution risks, coordinate with relevant departments, and facilitate contract signing. Organize project reviews to summarize experiences and lessons learned, ensuring continuous improvement and smooth progress of future projects. Lead project kick-off meetings and coordinate the transfer of bidding project documents to legal and project management teams for execution. Maintain customer relationships throughout project execution, manage contract payments and changes, and participate in project budget monitoring and control. Coordinate cross-functional teams including technology, procurement, project management, and legal departments to ensure successful bidding and negotiation outcomes. Demonstrate high self-discipline, resilience under stress, and self-motivation to sustain performance in high-intensity negotiations and long-term relationship management. Uphold a transparent, trustworthy, and stable professional attitude to foster consistent business results. Required competencies and certifications Minimum 5 years of experience in offshore engineering or shipbuilding industries with practical project operation expertise. At least 2 years of experience in regional market expansion with familiarity of business environments and cultural differences in target regions. Deep understanding of the upstream, midstream, and downstream industry chain, EPCIC general contracting models, and oil and gas market trends. Proficiency in independently building key customer maps for regional markets. Comprehensive knowledge of international bidding processes (RFQ/EOI → Clarification → Technical/Communication Proposal → Final Offer) and all major engineering life cycle stages to anticipate and mitigate execution risks during business development. Understanding of offshore engineering equipment design, construction processes, and industry standards for FPSO, FLNG, FPU, Jack-up, WHP, and PP. Familiarity with EPCIC contract frameworks and ability to identify key business risks including liquidated damages, letters of intent, interface issues, changes, tax, insurance, and local compliance. Capability to identify genuine project opportunities through exhibitions, consultants, partners, and internal resources. Preferred competencies and qualifications Proven ability to coordinate multiple departments to complete bidding and negotiation processes effectively. Strong professional attitude characterized by transparency, trustworthiness, and stability under pressure.

title: Sales Manager (1 Year Contract)
description: About Singapore Red Cross Academy (SRCA) The Singapore Red Cross Academy is the training arm of the Singapore Red Cross, dedicated to building community resilience by equipping individuals and organisations with essential humanitarian knowledge and skills. We are the region's go-to institute for humanitarian studies, offering a comprehensive suite of courses in First Aid & Resuscitation, Psychosocial Support, and other humanitarian and caregiving programmes. We strive to empower individuals and communities to help save lives. Job Purpose The job incumbent will be responsible for driving the sales performance of the Singapore Red Cross Academy's range of courses and training programmes to corporate clients, government agencies, educational institutions, and the general public. This role requires a dynamic and results-driven individual with a passion for humanitarian work and a proven track record in achieving ambitious sales targets. Remarks: Competitive basic pay plus commission/ incentive based on performance. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales Strategy and Execution: Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed course enrolment and revenue targets for SRCA. Identify new business opportunities and target markets for SRCA's training offerings, particularly within the corporate and institutional sectors. Conduct market research to stay abreast of industry trends, competitor activities, and pricing strategies. Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and tender submissions to potential clients. 2. Client Relationship Management (CRM): Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key corporate accounts and institutional partners. Act as the primary point of contact for large-scale training enquiries and customised corporate training solutions. Ensure high levels of client satisfaction and secure repeat business and referrals. 3. Sales Operations and Reporting: Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from lead generation and qualification to negotiation and closing. Maintain accurate records of sales activities, pipelines, and forecasts using the CRM system. Prepare regular sales reports and dashboards for management, detailing performance against targets and key metrics. Work closely with the Operations and Marketing teams to ensure seamless course delivery and effective promotional campaigns. 4. Product Knowledge and Advocacy: Maintain a deep understanding of all SRCA courses, accreditations (e.g., SRFAC, MOM), and subsidy schemes (e.g., SkillsFuture). Act as an ambassador for the Singapore Red Cross humanitarian mission and the value of its training programmes. 5. Team Collaboration (if applicable to team structure): Mentor and provide guidance to junior sales or account management staff, fostering a high-performance sales culture. Collaborate with the Curriculum Development team to communicate market needs and client feedback for continuous course improvement. Job Requirements Qualifications and Experience: Minimum Diploma or Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field. Minimum 3 years of relevant sales experience. Experience in the training and education sector is a plus. Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas. Skills and Knowledge: Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Strong business acumen and ability to understand client training needs. Proficiency in using CRM software and Microsoft Office Suite. Knowledge of Singapore's training landscape, including government grants and accreditation bodies, is highly advantageous. Demonstrates a high level of initiative, self-motivation, and integrity. Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.

title: Manager / Senior Manager, Events and Programmes
description: As part of the Events and Programmes team, you will be responsible for planning, coordinating, and executing Lifelong Learning Singapore’s (LLSG) events and outreach initiatives that inspire learning and participation in the SkillsFuture movement, while strengthening LLSG’s visibility. You will oversee the end-to-end delivery of key events, from conceptualisation and design to implementation and evaluation, ensuring each event/initiative engages individuals and enterprises, foster partnerships, and translate LLSG’s brand and mission into meaningful, memorable experiences. This role plays a pivotal part in driving public engagement, strengthening partnerships, and amplifying LLSG’s visibility through innovative, high-impact events and outreach programmes. Key Responsibilities Event Planning and Delivery Conceptualise, plan, and execute enterprise and public-facing events that advance LLSG’s outreach and engagement objectives. Manage end-to-end event logistics, budgeting, and vendor coordination to ensure seamless delivery. Contribute to the development of the annual events calendar and thematic programming. Event Design and Experience Design engaging, experiential formats that promote lifelong learning and align with LLSG’s brand identity. Drive creativity in event design, flow, design and content, integrating interactive, experiential and digital elements. Ensure events deliver clear value propositions and foster meaningful connections with LLSG’s community. Collaborative Partnerships Work closely with internal divisions (e.g. Communications, Digital Marketing, Partnerships) to ensure alignment and synergy in outreach initiatives. Engage external partners, agencies, and enterprises to co-create event activations and cross-promotional initiatives. Support partnership efforts that enhance event visibility, reach, and impact. Digital Integration & Analytics Collaborate with Communications and Digital Marketing team to leverage digital platforms for event outreach and engagement. Use analytics and performance metrics to assess event performance, audience engagement, and conversion rate. Apply data-driven insights to refine audience segmentation, event design and engagement strategies. Operations and Resource/Budget Management Track event KPIs and compile post-event reports to evaluate success, gather insights, and recommend improvements. Lead post-event debriefs and ensure learnings are documented and translated into actionable strategies. Develop and manage event budgets, ensuring cost efficiency and optimisation. Oversee resource allocation and expenditure, maintaining financial discipline while upholding quality standards. Qualifications and Requirements Background in Marketing, Communications, Events Management, Business, or a related field. Minimum 3–5 years of relevant experience in event management, outreach, or community engagement (public or private sector). Proven experience in managing medium to large-scale events or campaigns. Excellent coordination, project management, and stakeholder engagement skills. Creative, resourceful, and detail-oriented, with a strong sense of initiative. Familiarity with event management software, CRM, analytics dashboards, or hybrid event technologies is advantageous. Candidates who do not have the required qualification or relevant experience are welcome to apply. Successful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance and may be considered for an extension or be placed on a permanent tenure. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Senior/Lead Infra Consultant
description: NCS is a leading technology services firm that operates across the Asia Pacific region in over 20 cities, providing consulting, digital services, technology solutions, and more. We believe in harnessing the power of technology to achieve extraordinary things, creating lasting value and impact for our communities, partners, and people. Our diverse workforce of 13,000 has delivered large-scale, mission-critical, and multi-platform projects for governments and enterprises in Singapore and the APAC region. As a Senior/ Lead Consultant, IT Security (Client Information Security Lead), you will be responsible to proactively elevate the overall service quality to our Client while managing the necessary cyber security risk posture of NCS. What will you do? To support the LOB as an independent (i.e. Line 2) Info-security Advisory, Compliance Assessment and Security Incident Response Management. You will work as the interface between NCS Enterprise SBG practice and NCS Corporate HQ-wide cybersecurity leadership, driving organization-wide cyber security strategy, implementation, and in turn, ensuring client and practice requirements are fed back into the continual improvement of relevant strategy, policies and standards. To ensure cyber key risk indicators and controls are simplified, relevant and effective for risk monitoring, reporting and mitigation, and in compliance with NCS Cyber Security Policies, government legislation (e.g., IM8, Public Sector Data Security, PDPA) and good industry practice. Conduct independent internal risk assessment on NCS Client-facing projects to identify weakness and provide recommendations for improvement e.g. thematic assessment, Risk Mitigation and Corrective & Preventive Action Plan. Alignment with business’ leaders to better understand the business’ goals and to make appropriate security controls into the environment considering people, processes and automation. Act as a cyber security incident response advisor on potential security matters. Partner as Subject Matter Expert (SME) with key business stakeholders including Risk Management, Legal, NCS IT, Cyber Incident Response Team, People & Culture and any external institutions (e.g. PDPC) that are critical to the success of cyber security. To drive the cyber security program and to improve cyber awareness for data security and its impact on the organization. To enhance efficiency and improve reliability of operational security compliance and monitoring process using automation mechanism e.g. scripts and tooling. The ideal candidate should possess: Degree/Diploma or higher in Computer Science, Information Systems or equivalent At least one industry recognized security certification is, such as Certified Information Security Management (CISM), Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP) 5+ years of hands-on experience in information/cyber security risk assessment practice e.g. in the domains of Governance, Risk & Compliance, Secured-design Engineering, Cyber Security Operations, Incident Response, and/or Vulnerability Management In-depth working knowledge of cybersecurity risk assessment, thematic audit, data or security governance framework e.g. NIST, CIS20, IM8, etc Knowledge of information security principles, IM8, ISO 27001 controls, Center for Internet Security (CIS) controls, Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM) controls Hands-on experience with hybrid cloud infrastructure or system security, security technologies and tooling, e.g., identity access management, remote access management, endpoint security, network security & administration, and application security e.g. OWASP 10 (API, web, LLM, mobile) Added advantage with experience in security incident response, infrastructure/application penetration testing, bug bounty program, vulnerability assessment, monitoring of secrets in public repository, and cloud security operation Senior stakeholder management and working across various parts of the organization Team player with good interpersonal and communication skills both written and verbal Eye for details We are driven by our AEIOU beliefs—Adventure, Excellence, Integrity, Ownership, and Unity—and we seek individuals who embody these values in both their professional and personal lives. We are committed to our Impact: Valuing our clients, Growing our people, and Creating our future. Together, we make the extraordinary happen. Learn more about us at ncs.co and visit our LinkedIn career site.

title: COURSE DEVELOPER
description: AA International is seeking a talented and creative Course Developer to join our team. The Course Developer will play a key role in designing, developing, and implementing engaging and effective learning experiences for our clients. This role offers the opportunity to collaborate with subject matter experts to create high-quality courses that meet the needs of our target audience and support our company's mission and goals. Responsibilities: Collaborate with subject matter experts, instructional designers, and other stakeholders to identify learning objectives, content requirements, and course outcomes. Design and develop course content, instructional materials, and assessments using a variety of instructional design models, principles, and technologies. Create engaging and interactive learning experiences that incorporate multimedia elements, such as videos, animations, simulations, and interactive exercises. Write clear and concise instructional content, learning objectives, and assessments that align with course objectives and target audience needs. Conduct quality assurance checks and review course content for accuracy, consistency, and alignment with instructional design standards and best practices. Stay up-to-date with emerging trends, technologies, and best practices in instructional design and e-learning development, and recommend new approaches and strategies to enhance course effectiveness. Communicate effectively with stakeholders and project team members to provide updates on project status, solicit feedback, and address any issues or concerns. Assist with the evaluation and assessment of course effectiveness, gather learner feedback, and make recommendations for course improvements and revisions. Qualifications: Minimum Diploma in any discipline. Possess WSQ Diploma in Design and Development of Learning for Performance (DDDLP) or WSQ Diploma in Adult and Continuing Education (DACE) will be an advantage. Experience in course development, or curriculum design, preferably in public training courses. title: Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design
description: School of Art, Design and Media College of Computing and Data Science Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design The School of Art, Design and Media (ADM) at Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU) invites applications for a one-year Visiting Professor position in Games and/or Animation. ADM is part of the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (COHASS). This position is intended for eminent overseas academics such as international prize recipients or distinguished scholars whose presence will enhance the academic standing of the faculty. The primary responsibility of this role is teaching. Therefore, candidates should be able to demonstrate a strong teaching track record in a current or previous Associate Professor academic level position. We seek candidates with expertise in one or more areas of Games and/or Animation, including fields such as game design, interactive media, concept development, animation, and creative technologies. The appointed faculty will play a key role in supporting ADM’s position as a leader in contemporary digital media and design education in the region. Located in multi-cultural Singapore, the School of Art Design Media (ADM) in Nanyang Technological University (NTU) offers a studio-based education in a comprehensive university setting, providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to develop and produce groundbreaking creative works and scholarly activity. ADM also promotes Singapore’s creative culture and identity and is positioned to play a central role in the transformation of Singapore into a regional center of media and creativity. Designed and taught by international faculty drawn from nearly 20 countries, the school offer 4-year BFA programs in Design Art and Media Art, with an adaptive and evolving curriculum building upon excellence in our core disciplines of Animation, Film, Game, Photography, Interaction, Product Design, and Visual Communication, The programs are designed to meet the highest professional and academic standards worldwide and are responsive to student interests and evolving global needs. Post-graduate programs combine theory and practice, offering MA and PhD by research and a MA in Museum and Curatorial Practice. In 2026 ADM will start an innovative MSc Game Design Art & Technology, taught jointly with the College of Computer and Data Sciences (CCDS). ADM ranks amongst the top 5 art and design schools in Asia and top 20 worldwide (QS World University Rankings by subject 2025). NTU is a research-intensive university, home to 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in Engineering, Science, Humanities, Social Science, Business, Education and Medicine. Ranked amongst the world’s top 30 universities by QS, NTU has also been named amongst the world’s top young universities for the past 7 years. Further information about the University and the School can be viewed at the following websites: NTU : www.ntu.edu.sg ADM : www.ntu.edu.sg/adm Applicants must be confident navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of digital media and technology and demonstrate a strong commitment to teaching innovation and student mentorship. The role is teaching-focused; applicants should bring an established record of tertiary-level teaching experience and curricular engagement. As a creative practitioner and educator with expertise in animation, games, or interactive media, candidate will demonstrate: A strong record in tertiary education (A terminal MFA (or its equivalent) or PhD degree in a relevant field is required), with the ability to guide student-led projects from concept to completion across games, animation, visual development, or interactive media. · A recognised practice in animation, games, or related fields, with an impactful portfolio showcasing innovation, storytelling, and design across emerging technologies such as AR/VR, motion graphics, generative AI, or installations. · Evidence of scholarly output in contemporary forums related to games, animation, or creative computing, and a commitment to advancing the discipline through research or creative practice. · A willingness to engage with cultural, ethical, and philosophical dimensions of media practice, and the ability to connect with fields like digital humanities, creative coding, or social platforms. · A passion for education and the ability to foster a supportive, inclusive, and diverse learning environment, with strong interpersonal and organizational skills. · National or international recognition by academic or industry, with a track record of leadership and contribution to the evolution of games, animation, or interactive media. Applicants are invited to submit their applications through the NTU career portal by clicking on the “Apply” button above. Please provide the following in your application: 1. Cover letter; 2. Curriculum vitae (including records of competitive grants and graduate supervision, significant creative works and scholarly publications; if applicable); 3. Teaching statement; 4. Research statement; The closing date for applications is 26 September 2025. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified via email.

title: Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance)
description:
​ Our Client is an established company in
Singapore, who is seeking to recruit a Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance). Lead Cybersecurity Specialist
(Governance) You will be the primary
architect of the security governance and risk management framework., with the mission
to transform GRC from a compliance-heavy exercise into a strategic enabler. You
will ensure that risk management is deeply integrated into the lifecycle of
every digital system, from web applications to critical Operational Technology
(OT) environments. 1. Enterprise Risk
Governance & Management ·       Dynamic Risk Registers: Establish and oversee the security
risk register. You will ensure that registers are not static documents but
"living" tools that accurately reflect the current threat landscape
and project status across all agencies. ·       Senior Management Facilitation: Lead and facilitate
high-level risk conversations with Senior Management. You must be able to
translate complex technical risks into clear business impacts to drive informed
resource allocation and prioritisation. ·       Risk Analysis Framework: Develop a robust framework to guide
agencies in performing consistent, high-quality risk analysis. This framework
should empower agencies to take calculated risks for innovation rather than
defaulting to "no" due to risk aversion. 2. Threat Risk Assessment
(TRA) & Standards ·       Unified TRA Framework: Establish and maintain standards for
conducting Threat Risk Assessments across diverse domains, including Cloud
(GCC), Web Applications, and OT/ICS systems. ·       Crown Jewel Identification: Develop SOPs to guide project
teams in identifying "Crown Jewels" (Critical Information Assets) and
mapping comprehensive threat vectors. ·       Standardisation of Controls: Define common security
configuration standards and ensure that controls are technically effective in
mitigating identified risks, rather than just meeting baseline requirements. 3. Zero Trust &
Architecture Governance ·       Zero Trust Roadmap: Lead the establishment of a Zero Trust
Framework, setting the standards for identity-based security,
micro-segmentation, and "never trust, always verify" architectures. ·       Architectural Advisory: Provide expert GRC input during the
design phase of high-impact systems to ensure security-by-design and alignment
with standards. ·       Technology Application: Evaluate and recommend security
technologies that effectively mitigate specific risks, ensuring that defensive
layers remain relevant against modern threats. 4. Supply Chain &
Ecosystem Risk Management ·       Third-Party Risk Strategy: Establish the framework for
managing risks across the software supply chain and IT vendors. ·       Dependency & Vendor Risk: Develop standards for assessing
the cyber-resilience of third-party partners and managing risks associated with
software dependencies (e.g., Open Source libraries). 5. Audit Excellence &
Systemic Improvement ·       Proactive Readiness: Shift agencies from "reactive"
audit preparation to a state of continuous compliance and readiness. ·       Root Cause Rectification: Oversee the closure of audit
findings, ensuring agencies implement substantive, effective technical fixes
rather than surface-level measures. 6. Stakeholder Management
& Threat Intelligence ·       Education & Advocacy: Partner with key stakeholders to
inculcate a proactive risk management mindset. ·       Threat & Tech Foresight: Keep abreast of evolving Actor
TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures) and technology changes. Periodically
review the relevancy of existing defences against the latest threats. Requirements Experience ·       Years of Experience: 10 to 12 years in Cybersecurity GRC,
Information Security Risk Management, or Security Architecture. ·       Domain Breadth: Proven experience in managing risks across IT
and Cloud environments; exposure to OT (Operational Technology) systems is a
significant advantage. ·       Regulatory Knowledge: Deep familiarity with security policies
(e.g., Instruction Manual on IT Management) and international standards (e.g.,
NIST, ISO 27001). Technical Skills ·       Risk Methodologies: Mastery of risk assessment methodologies
(e.g., TVRA) and the ability to translate technical vulnerabilities into
business risk. ·       Security Technologies: Strong technical understanding of
various Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) components and cloud security
technologies. Such as Firewalls, EDR, IAM, SIEM, CSPM, CWPP, CASB and secrets
management etc. ·       Threat Awareness: Ability to map technical controls to the
MITRE ATT&CK framework to ensure defensive coverage. ·       Offensive Security: Proficiency in manual and automated
testing tools; deep understanding of the MITRE ATT&CK framework and common
TTPs. ·       Certifications: Professional certifications such as CISM
(Certified Information Security Manager), CRISC (Certified in Risk and
Information Systems Control), CISSP, OSCP or OSWE (Offensive Security Web
Expert) are highly preferred. Soft Skills ·       Strategic Influence: Ability to educate and persuade senior
stakeholders (CIOs/Project Owners) on the importance of rigorous risk
governance. ·       Critical Thinking: Ability to look past surface-level audit
compliance to find and fix underlying systemic issues. ·       Lifelong Learner: A genuine passion for staying updated on
the latest security technologies and evolving cyber threat landscapes. ·       Risk Articulation: Exceptional ability to
"translate" deep technical issues (e.g., zero-day vulnerabilities,
configuration drifts) into business risk for non-technical senior executives. JJ Consulting Services EA Licence No.: 12C6207 Applicants are invited to send in a MS Word resume to jobs@jjconsulting.com.sg stating
position applying for/present/expected salaries and earliest available date. We thank all applicants in advance and regret that only short listed candidates
will be notified.

title: Manager (Operations)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The Manager (Operations) supports the effective, safe, and efficient management of LKYSPP’s physical environment at NUS Bukit Timah Campus(BTC), acting as a key operational steward and interface between the School and NUS central units on facilities, space, and building-related matters. Beyond ensuring operational continuity, the role calls for sound judgement, ownership, and the ability to think holistically—connecting policy constraints, operational realities, user needs, and longer-term considerations. The incumbent is expected to proactively observe, assess, and suggest practical improvements that enhance functionality, comfort, sustainability, and user experience, while ensuring timely execution and responsible use of resources. 1. Facilities and Vendor Management • Oversee general maintenance of office, teaching, and student spaces within School-managed areas at BTC. • Coordinate periodic maintenance and improvement works, ensuring alignment with NUS standards, policies, and approvals. • Manage School-appointed vendors and contractors, and coordinate effectively with NUS-appointed vendors where applicable. 2. Asset and Inventory Management • Manage the asset lifecycle for furniture, equipment, and fittings in compliance with NUS policies. • Maintain accurate records to support operational planning, audits, and space decisions. 3. Access Management • Administer key press and card access systems for School spaces, in coordination with NUS security frameworks. • Manage access issuance, retrieval, and transitions responsibly during staff and space changes. 4. Operations and Logistics • Plan and coordinate office relocations, classroom reconfigurations, furniture shifting, and space reorganisations. • Join the dots across stakeholders, timelines, and constraints to ensure changes are executed smoothly and promptly. 5. Project Management and A&A Coordination • Coordinate renovation, retrofitting, and A&A projects affecting School spaces, working closely with NUS central infrastructure units (e.g. UCI) and external consultants Qualifications • Bachelor’s degree in Facilities Management, Building Management, Engineering, Operations Management, or a related field. Experience • At least 5 years of relevant experience in facilities, operations, or infrastructure management, preferably within a centrally governed campus, institutional, or complex organisational setting. Key Attributes, Knowledge & Skills • Strong sense of stewardship and responsibility over shared physical environments. • Demonstrated ability to think, observe, connect issues, and propose improvements, rather than merely executing instructions. • A “home-improvement” mindset: takes pride in maintaining and improving spaces that people use daily. • Genuine interest in creating conducive environments through physical design, space planning, technology enablement, greenery, and environmental considerations. • Appreciation of sustainable campus practices and long-term maintainability. • Sound judgement in balancing prudence, cost, functionality, and quality, within centrally owned infrastructure. • Ability to work effectively with central policy owners and multiple stakeholders, and to get things done in a timely manner.
