Adherence to secure coding practices to mitigate vulnerabilities, protect sensitive data, and ensure secure software solutions. Implementation of effective unit testing practices to ensure proper code design, readability, and reliability. Analyst Expectations To perform prescribed activities in a timely manner and to a high standard consistently driving continuous improvement. Requires in-depth technical knowledge and experience in their assigned area of expertise Thorough understanding of the underlying principles and concepts within the area of expertise They lead and supervise a team, guiding and supporting professional development, allocating work requirements and coordinating team resources. If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. OR for an individual contributor, they develop technical expertise in work area, acting as an advisor where appropriate. Will have an impact on the work of related teams within the area. Partner with other functions and business areas. Takes responsibility for end results of a team’s operational processing and activities. Escalate breaches of policies / procedure appropriately. Take responsibility for embedding new policies/ procedures adopted due to risk mitigation. Advise and influence decision making within own area of expertise. Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work you own or contribute to. Deliver your work and areas of responsibility in line with relevant rules, regulation and codes of conduct. Maintain and continually build an understanding of how own sub-function integrates with function, alongside knowledge of the organisations products, services and processes within the function. Demonstrate understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function. Make evaluative judgements based on the analysis of factual information, paying attention to detail. Resolve problems by identifying and selecting solutions through the application of acquired technical experience and will be guided by precedents. Guide and persuade team members and communicate complex / sensitive information. Act as contact point for stakeholders outside of the immediate function, while building a network of contacts outside team and external to the organisation. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave. Plan resources, budgets, and policies; manage and maintain policies/ processes; deliver continuous improvements and escalate breaches of policies/procedures. They may also lead a number of specialists to influence the operations of a department, in alignment with strategic as well as tactical priorities, while balancing short and long term goals and ensuring that budgets and schedules meet corporate requirements. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. Manage and mitigate risks through assessment, in support of the control and governance agenda. Demonstrate leadership and accountability for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work your team does. Demonstrate comprehensive understanding of the organisation functions to contribute to achieving the goals of the business. Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategies. Create solutions based on sophisticated analytical thought comparing and selecting complex alternatives. In-depth analysis with interpretative thinking will be required to define problems and develop innovative solutions. Adopt and include the outcomes of extensive research in problem solving processes. We are seeking a Senior Java developer for designing and developing ultra-low-latency end-to-end Direct Market Access (DMA) trading platform. Developing a pragmatic software solution as per the requirement specifications. Unit / system / regression / performance testing of the solutions. Fixing bugs coming out of System / UAT testing. Reviewing code produced by others. Supporting the implementation of each new code. Resolving any post-implementation issues as needed. Advanced expertise in computer networks, computer architecture, and operating systems like Linux. title: Senior Transaction Monitoring & Sanctions Specialist
description: Job Purpose You will be responsible for overseeing transaction monitoring activities, investigating suspicious transactions, and ensuring compliance with MAS regulations, AML/CFT requirements, and sanctions obligations. The role also involves providing guidance to analysts and business units, improving transaction monitoring controls, and supporting regulatory and audit engagements. Key Responsibilities Review, investigate and assess transaction monitoring alerts to identify potential money laundering, terrorism financing, or other financial crime risks. Handle and resolve escalated alerts from analysts, ensuring investigations are thorough, well-documented, and completed within agreed timelines. Review and clear sanctions-related alerts, including enhanced due diligence where required. Prepare, review and submit Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) to the relevant authorities. Liaise with business units to obtain information, provide transaction monitoring advisory, and support case investigations. Handle production orders, seizure orders, and related requests from local authorities. Conduct quality assurance reviews, including periodic sample checks on alert clearance and documentation standards. Review and recommend enhancements to transaction monitoring thresholds, scenarios, and parameters. Establish, review and update transaction monitoring policies, procedures, and controls in line with MAS Notices and Guidelines. Perform gap analysis on new or updated MAS regulatory requirements and support implementation. Provide training, guidance and ongoing support to analysts and relevant business units. Respond to queries from MAS, auditors, and other external stakeholders. Participate in audits, regulatory inspections, and internal control reviews. Carry out any other duties as assigned by the Team Lead, Head of Department, or Management. Job Requirements Education Recognised Bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Business, Finance, Accounting, or a related discipline. Experience Minimum 4 years of experience in the banking or financial services industry . At least 2 years of hands-on experience in Transaction Monitoring or AML investigations. Knowledge & Skills Strong understanding of transaction monitoring, AML/CFT risks, and banking fund flows. Working knowledge of sanctions screening and trade finance enhanced due diligence is preferred. Experience in investigation, case documentation, and STR preparation. Exposure to transaction monitoring threshold or parameter tuning is an advantage. Strong analytical and investigative skills with excellent attention to detail. Good written communication skills, particularly in report writing. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.

title: Chief Commercial Officer (International Education)
description: About the Role This is a newly created Executive Leadership position with a strong regional growth mandate, with particular emphasis on the China and ASEAN markets. As SagePaths Global Education continues its expansion across Asia, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will play a pivotal role in shaping the Group’s commercial strategy, driving sustainable revenue growth, and strengthening its international education ecosystem. The CCO will be responsible for building a scalable, data-driven, and high-performance commercial framework that integrates student recruitment, admissions, marketing, partnerships, and digital systems across all business units. This role is instrumental in translating strategic vision into commercial success and long-term market leadership. About SagePaths Global Education SagePaths Global Education is a leading regional education group headquartered in Singapore, operating a diversified portfolio of education and education-related businesses. Our brands include SagePaths Global Education, Singapore Education Development Centre, SagePartners Business Consultancy, SOVO Student Apartment, Pickering Institute, Pickering Global Campus, as well as a growing network of Overseas Teaching Centres across China. We specialise in building a fully integrated education ecosystem that supports international students across their entire academic journey, including: International student placement and recruitment Cross-business services centred around international students Student accommodation development and operations Academic pathways and university articulation programmes Foundation and Diploma programmes Online and blended education delivery Transnational education and cross-border programme delivery Our vision is to build a globally connected education ecosystem that empowers students through high-quality, accessible, and future-oriented learning pathways. We work closely with universities, private education institutions, and overseas partners to deliver well-recognised academic programmes and meaningful international collaboration opportunities. As we enter our next phase of strategic development, we are strengthening our Executive Leadership Team to support rapid regional expansion and organisational scaling. The Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will be a core member of this leadership group, working directly with the Managing Director and the Board to lead the Group’s commercial transformation, revenue growth strategy, and international market expansion. This is a rare opportunity to shape the commercial architecture of a fast-growing international education group and to make a lasting impact on its regional and global footprint. Key Responsibilities Reporting directly to the Managing Director, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will lead the Group’s end-to-end commercial strategy, with full responsibility across: Marketing, branding, and corporate communications for all SagePaths and Pickering brands Student recruitment strategy, sales performance management, admissions operations, and conversion optimisation Agent network development, channel management, and strategic partnerships (both domestic and international) Commercial systems architecture, including CRM, websites, digital marketing platforms, and data analytics infrastructure Pricing strategy, revenue diversification initiatives, and market expansion planning The role carries executive accountability to: Drive sustainable enrolment growth and strong revenue performance across both onshore and offshore markets Build a predictable, data-driven recruitment pipeline supported by disciplined sales governance and reporting structures Optimise admissions workflows, conversion rates, and overall student acquisition efficiency Lead CRM and digital platform integration to deliver a seamless, consistent, and high-quality student recruitment experience Strengthen commercial compliance with Singapore PEI regulations (SSG, EduTrust), overseas regulatory frameworks, and partner contractual obligations Champion an exceptional student experience from first engagement through to enrolment, consistently applied across all business units Develop and execute a customer-centric, brand-driven marketing strategy aligned with SagePaths’ premium and professional positioning Build, manage, and scale strategic partnerships with universities, private education institutions, overseas schools, and pathway providers Establish scalable commercial processes, structures, and performance frameworks that support rapid regional and international growth The Successful Candidate As a key member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CCO will work closely with the Managing Director, Executive Dean, and Operations to translate strategic direction into measurable and sustainable commercial performance. We are seeking a commercially astute, internationally minded leader with: A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Commerce, or a related discipline; postgraduate qualifications are strongly preferred 10 years of senior commercial leadership experience, MUST within international education, transnational education, or higher education services Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin Chinese is essential , with the ability to conduct professional-level communication, negotiations, and relationship management with business partners, universities, and partner institutions across Chin Proven experience leading large-scale student recruitment, admissions, and marketing operations across multiple markets Deep understanding of international education ecosystems, student mobility trends, agent networks, and education partnerships Strong familiarity with compliance frameworks, including Singapore PEI / EduTrust requirements and international regulatory standards A strong track record in building, scaling, and optimising CRM systems, digital marketing platforms, and performance dashboards Experience integrating CRM with LMS, finance, admissions, and student management systems A strategic mindset combined with strong execution capability and operational discipline Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to build high-performing, values-driven teams across marketing, recruitment, and admissions Comfort and effectiveness operating within a multi-country, multi-brand, and fast-growth environment Regular travel across Singapore, China, and Southeast Asia will be required to support regional operations and partnerships.

title: Head of Membership, Asia
description: POSITION PURPOSE The Head of Membership, Asia is part of the CASE Global membership team. This position is responsible for membership growth, engagement, and retention across Asia, including key markets such as South East Asia (e.g. Singapore, Malaysia), Hong Kong and China, India, and West Asia (e.g., Lebanon and the U.A.E.). This includes, but is not limited to, account management of member institutions, managing membership products and services, and the regional strategy for acquisition, engagement, and retention of members. The position is accountable for the identification and cultivation of sponsorship opportunities that support strategic priorities and regional programs. The position works closely with partners, volunteers, and internal teams to build lasting relationships and advance regional impact. In a small office, all staff are expected to be involved in developing, supporting, delivering and driving all CASE business. This role has responsibility for membership revenue, including meeting established targets for growth and retention of members. The core functions of this position include but are not limited to the following: Membership Strategy Lead the Asia-Pacific membership retention and growth strategy in Asia, working closely with the Asia-Pacific Executive Director and colleagues across the Global Membership team to achieve targets identified in CASE’s Global Membership Growth Plan. Identify and build relationships with potential partners to support membership engagement and growth. Maintain responsibility for achieving an annual revenue goal and identifying opportunities for new revenue streams. In consultation with the Senior Director, Member Strategy and Marketing, manage the membership acquisition strategy, with particular emphasis on emerging markets and identifying potential new markets. Membership Engagement : Develop and own membership retention plan to improve retention rate across the membership in Asia. Manage the provision of the first line of customer service for all membership enquiries, meeting internal standards for response time and quality of response. Working with the Executive Director, AP and the Senior Director, Member Strategy and Marketing, develop and manage a regional membership stewardship programme, including regular touch points, to improve engagement with particular emphasis on at-risk members Manage an institutional or individual onboarding plan for new members Volunteer Stewardship Establish good relationships with volunteer leaders in Asia to ensure goals and outcomes support CASE’s priorities and align with our strategic direction. Lead the strategic recruitment of new volunteers and coordinate induction activities in Asia. Support volunteers in their roles in fulfilling engagement activities, networking activities, and other projects between meetings. Liaise with colleagues responsible for educational programmes in the Asia-Pacific region and in the global Membership, Volunteerism, and Engagement Division to align activities and deliver a consistent volunteer experience in Asia. Fiscal Management/Budget Responsibilities/Judicious Use of Resources Manage the annual budget assigned for the role and prepare periodic reports as requested. Exercise fiscal responsibility in the utilisation of all CASE resources. Liaison Responsibilities Work cooperatively and diplomatically with all divisions and regions. Maintain flexibility to work across different time-zones, including late evenings, as and when required. Liaise with CASE colleagues in other parts of the world, especially those in membership, marketing, communications, and volunteer engagement, to ensure the best sharing of ideas, expertise, and knowledge. Incorporate tasks and/or projects related to objectives as directed by the CASE Strategic Plan. Image Responsibilities In keeping with the CASE Values Statement, ensures that CASE is well represented by exercising a high degree of professionalism, accuracy, and ethics, in all activities, services, and products. POSITION REQUIREMENTS Education & Experience Educated to degree level. Fluency and competency in writing in English. Proficiency in a relevant regional language strongly preferred (e.g., Mandarin, Cantonese, Arabic or Hindi). Evidence of business development and/or marketing experience to deliver against income and/or numerical targets at a team level and independently Evidence of managing a team, budgets, and targets. Experience in institutional advancement, e.g., alumni relations, communications, fundraising, or advancement services, preferred. Skills & Abilities A demonstrated interest in advancing education in the nonprofit sector. A strong commitment to contributing to an inclusive organizational culture. An understanding and interest in engaging with a broad range of cultures and countries. Excellent relationship-building, account management and member services skills, demonstrating evidence of working with and developing opportunities for volunteer, supporter and/or member engagement Excellent time management and prioritization skills with ability to manage multiple projects. High comfort level with IT systems and good working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (including Access), Outlook and Database Management. Ability to communicate effectively with people of all levels and work well as part of a team. Self-starter with ability to take ownership of and work autonomously on initiatives. Strong commercial, market-led approach. Ability and willingness to travel 2-3 times a year in the region. Readiness to work unsocial hours on occasion (e.g., over weekends at conferences). Maintain a high standard of excellence. Ability to embrace CASE values and work collaboratively with enthusiasm and energy.

title: Actuarial (Financial Services) Consultant, Risk Consulting
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The Opportunity As part of our life actuarial team, you’ll focus on high-impact opportunities to apply your knowledge and experience that will shape our services. You’ll build valuable relationships with clients and develop strong capabilities, through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues. You will be at the forefront of issues that are shaping the insurance industry in Asia and gain a wide exposure to a diverse range of actuarial projects. Your Key Responsibilities As a Senior Consultant in the Actuarial team, you will be assisting and leading the delivery of a wide range of client engagements, leading or working as part of a project team, relating to IFRS 17, Appointed Actuary work, actuarial valuation, pricing and product development, actuarial modelling, Merger & Acquisitions (M&A) and due diligence. You will also be performing evaluation in areas related to financial risk, product distribution and capital issues. You will also work with advanced financial modeling approaches and systems, e.g. designing valuation and Asset Liability Management (ALM) frameworks. Skills and Attributes for Success Good understanding of economic or market issues and the ability to interpret their impact on clients Possess strong interpersonal and teaming skills Has strong analytical and creative skills Leverage technology to continually learn, improve service delivery and maintain our leading-edge best practices To Qualify for the role, you must have Newly or Nearly qualified with either the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (UK), the Society of Actuaries (US) or the Institute of Actuaries of Australia Added advantage for candidate with experience in the life actuarial team or an actuarial consulting firm Good knowledge of Prophet software Good written and verbal communication skills Ideally, you’ll also have Depth of experience in at least one of the following areas: Financial modeling Financial and regulatory reporting Product and distribution strategy ERM / Solvency II M&A due diligence What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. What working at EY offers We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. We also offer you: Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

title: Vice President, International Credit Division (LBO team) (Job ID: 10074594)
description: Overview of the Department The International Credit Division (ICD) function in APAC evaluates and sanctions transaction proposals submitted by the Business and monitors counterparty credit risk. APAC covers all Asia including Australia, New Zealand, Korea, China, Hong Kong and Taiwan. ICD Credit Managers engage with APAC Business colleagues to evaluate business opportunities and credit submissions. Throughout the process ICD supports and advises the Business on due diligence, structuring and documentation requirements to mitigate credit risk. Credit Managers also risk manage and are accountable for their allocated portfolio of names and contribute to portfolio reporting. ICD covers a diverse range of credit and market risk products and counterparties including financial institutions, corporate counterparties, sovereign entities and funds. ICD also contributes and plays a role in the setting of risk appetite and formulation of policy in conjunction with other internal stakeholders. Main Purpose of the Role To be responsible for analysing and writing recommendations for transaction proposals to private equity led leveraged buyout (LBO) obligors. Potential and existing deals tend to be complex and will require early engagement and close cooperation with the Business, including the Leverage and Acquisition Finance team. This engagement is to support and guide the Business in respect to risk appetite, due diligence, structuring and financial modelling. This is an important hire within ICD APAC and the role requires a high calibre individual who can take on a high level of responsibility with minimum supervision and has the capability to progress within the organisation. Ability to deliver within tight deadlines is critical. Key Responsibilities Providing solutions and adding value to the Business via discussions, due diligence, giving clear guidance on policy and risk appetite, and influencing transaction structures and facility terms & conditions to mitigate risk. Present transactions in a concise and clear manner to senior Business and ICD management both locally and overseas for their approval, evidencing deep knowledge of the client, transaction, and risks & mitigants. Provide clearly articulated views on whether a client and proposal is bankable and if not, where appropriate, providing alternatives and risk mitigating solutions. Attend internal deal screening committees and communicate credit views and challenges on proposals. Evaluation of credit applications, internal borrower ratings and assumptions used for cashflow forecasts for new or existing corporate clients in APAC. This includes reviewing transaction material and the analysis submitted by the Business, where appropriate challenging assumptions and conclusions. Proactively track and monitor allocated portfolio including, but not limited to Leveraged Finance accounts from a counterparty, sector and country perspective, and where issues are identified, elevating these to internal stakeholders. Work with the Business to formulate mitigating actions where material risk issues arise. Support new initiatives by the Business providing guidance from a risk perspective. Involvement where appropriate in the development of credit and policy guidelines and participation and support on assigned projects including contribution to change initiatives to drive efficiencies. Work collegiately with all stakeholders across divisions, building a network of trusted relationships. Active involvement in term sheet, loan documentation and ISDA/CSA review and discussions with Business stakeholders, influencing credit terms where appropriate, ensuring these are acceptable from a risk and commercial standpoint. Job Requirements At least 10 years of corporate credit analysis and structuring experience within the banking/Big 4/ advisory consultancy industry. Requires product experience in the analysis and terms of traditional corporate lending and Leveraged Finance, including LBO/MBO’s and structured transactions. Experience in risk analysis and formulating recommendations on credit proposals across a broad range of client types, including corporates and private equity across various sectors and countries in APAC Good understanding of different sector risk dynamics, drivers and fundamentals, with an ability to apply this in the analysis of counterparty success factors and business and financial risk. Strong track record of working closely with Business partners, building trust and adding value by providing clear and early advice on risk appetite, due diligence requirements and structuring, and finding solutions to risk issues. Track record of providing a contribution beyond credit risk management, including on credit portfolio reporting, deep dive analysis, contribution to policy, special projects, and ideas for the improvement of process, quality and frameworks. Evidencing a strong graduate or post graduate academic record. Logical writing skills. Personal & Other Requirements: Excellent written and verbal communication considering the audience and any cultural sensitivities. Communicates verbally in a confident and effective manner to stakeholders at any level. Deep analytical skills and the ability to think laterally to find solutions and mitigation to risk issues. Ability to see the big picture on proposals and identify key risks and due diligence required. Ability to make clear recommendations balancing all risk and commercial considerations. Strong problem solving and decision-making skills and sound judgement. Understands the external market and keeps up with market trends. Decisive individual but listens and weighs up all views before formulating an opinion. Shares and escalates risk issues proposing remediation plans. Strong team player focused on team results and success of the Business. Excellent interpersonal skills and builds trusted relationships. Resilient individual able to operate under pressure with urgency. High integrity and leads by example creating a constructive culture. description: Hydroinformatics Institute Pte. Ltd. ("H2i") are looking to strengthen its leadership team with a Senior Project Manager. This is a pivotal role for a professional capable of not only executing complex consultancy and technology development projects in water resources and environmental management but also playing a strategic role in shaping our Consulting business. You will act as a primary interface with our customers, fostering strong partnerships while holding them accountable to project prerequisites and timelines. You will provide leadership to cross-functional teams and contribute directly to the company’s strategic initiatives for growth and market leadership. Key Responsibilities ·      Strategic Leadership & Business Shaping o   Collaborate with the COO to refine delivery methodologies, improve Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and drive operational excellence within the division. o   Provide mentorship and leadership to cross-functional teams, fostering a culture of accountability and high performance. o   Proactively work with management to develop new business opportunities and lead the preparation of high-quality proposals for new projects. ·      Client Management & Accountability o   Act as the trusted advisor to clients while rigorously managing client-side dependencies. You will hold customers accountable for their deliverables (data, approvals, resources) to ensure project momentum. o   Lead difficult conversations regarding scope creep and change control, ensuring that project objectives remain aligned with commercial agreements. ·      Project Execution & Financial Control o   Execute and manage consultancy and technology development projects related to real-time water resource management (e.g., forecasting systems, water quality modelling) from initiation to post-completion handoff. o   Develop detailed project plans outlining scope, milestones, and resource allocations. Proactively identify risks and implement mitigation measures. o   Financial Management: Manage project budgets (> $1 million SGD) and cash flows, including processing payment claims, assessing financial impacts of changes, and ensuring projects are delivered within budget. o   Reporting: Maintain accurate data in the project management system and report project status to senior management on a bi-weekly and ad-hoc basis. Minimum Qualifications ·   Minimum 8-10 years of experience in managing consultancy and technology development projects, with a proven track record of delivering projects on time and within budget. ·      Master of Science degree in relevant field. ·      Must be legally eligible to work in Singapore. Singapore citizens and PRs preferred. Desirable Qualifications ·      Familiarity with working with government procurement procedures and executing projects in public sector domains. ·      Experience in managing projects with budgets exceeding $1million SGD. ·      Experience or affinity with real-time control applications and operational management systems (e.g. FEWS, MIKE Operations). ·     Experience or affinity in working with water modelling software packages (e.g. WFLOW, Sobek, Mike11, Delft 3D). ·      Experience or affinity in working with management/collaboration tools such as Confluence, Slack, Zoho and Gitlab. ·      PMP certification or equivalent. Skills and Competencies ·      Ability to navigate complex regulatory environments. ·      Excellent communication skills for stakeholder management and reporting progress updates within and outside of own domain. ·      Analytical skills for problem solving and risk mitigation. ·     Strong leadership skills to manage cross functional teams effectively.

title: Senior Fraud Analyst
description: Job Summary The role will be responsible for •	Leading the group team on mule and scam detection processes, rules across multiple SCB markets •	Leading the development of additional solutions to enhance detection rates or for better targeting of payments to be investigated to reduce customer friction. •	Evaluating the tools/Techniques proposed (internally or by external Vendors) to improve fraud detection. •	Leading database enhancement projects and communicate the same to internal technology teams to further facilitate leveraging existing data base to design rules, run simulations and detect anomalies. •	Designing rules/ strategies for mule and scam prevention using descriptive data analytics to understand mule and scam behaviour leveraging the data base and using R/SQL •	Fraud prevention strategies based on analysis to enhance fraud detection while keeping customer friction in check. •	Leading ongoing reviews of current rules/policies and developing optimization strategies. •	To analyse the data from various sources to ensure optimal strategy design. •	Communicating and presenting the analysis on mules and scams to multiple stakeholders in the organization including Audit, country teams and Group senior management •	Working with multiple cross functional teams to ensure error free and smooth implementation of designed fraud strategy. •	Effectively communicating plans/strategies to cross-functional team members and management. •	Ensuring proper documentation and following the policies and procedures while making strategy updates •	Ensuring solutions meet business needs and requirements. •	Prioritizing initiatives based on business needs and requirements. •	Serving as a liaison between stakeholders and users. •	To manage the relationship between country and other team members within FRSC. •	To provide Guidance to team members on analytical issues. Role Responsibilities •	Following the requirements, build new strategies in conjunction with the stakeholders to deliver first class fraud risk management framework •	Ensure the Fraud Risk Management framework is effectively embedded and communicated across WRB Customers segment. •	Develop and maintain fraud management capabilities, skills and infrastructure in the country that are following both internal policies and external regulations and are responsive to fraud attacks. •	Act quickly and decisively when any risk and control weakness becomes apparent and ensure they are addressed within an appropriate timeframe and escalated through the relevant committees •	Encourage and promote an active training and learning environment to facilitate excellence in risk management, risk awareness, behaviours, and accountability. •	Deliver the defined aspects of the Fraud Risk role to support the Group's Fraud risk management approach and objectives. •	Ensure that the Fraud risk role is managed in accordance with the defined Fraud Risk Framework and associated Policy and Standards; and those issues are identified, escalated, and addressed as appropriate. •	Establish strong ties into the relevant business lines governance, risk and control committees to ensure adequate monitoring, tracking and governance of Fraud risk. •	Drive integration of Fraud Risk Framework and associated Policy and Standards into business lines and utilise for the ongoing governance process established within the Bank. •	Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •	Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across the Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •	Lead the Fraud risk team to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles: [Fair Outcomes for Clients; Effective Financial Markets; Financial Crime Compliance; The Right Environment]. •	Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate, and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Our Ideal Candidate Skills & Experience •	10+ years of relevant working experience in Banking domain •	Deep knowledge of the digital fraud modus operandi and how these risks are mitigated •	Deep knowledge of the behaviour of money mules at different stages of the digital banking lifecycle •	Expertise in SAS/R/Python/SQL (Any one of them at least) •	Knowledge of Digital Forensics data and its usage •	Knowledge and experience of Analytics tools and techniques •	Exceptional analytical and conceptual thinking skills. •	Experience creating detailed reports •	The ability to influence stakeholders and work closely with them to determine acceptable solutions. •	Excellent communication skills. •	Excellent planning, organizational, and time management skills. •	Good understanding of the payment’s ecosystem including various payment types •	Ability to gather actionable insights from large volumes of data. •	Combination of hard and soft skills is required for this role. •	Stakeholder management at various levels within the Bank Qualifications •	Bachelor’s degree in Data Science or Computer Science Role Specific Technical Competencies •	Technical Skills – Business Analysis •	Technical Skills - Delivery •	Technical Skills - Security •	Technical Skills – Data Analytics About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: Industrial Property Consultant
description: About the Role We are seeking a motivated and analytical individual to join our consultancy team as an Industrial Property Consultant . In this role, you will work closely with industrial clients to develop a thorough understanding of their business operations, financial position, workforce profile and operational requirements. This understanding will be applied to support property-related decisions that have direct implications on the client’s operations, cost structure and long-term planning. The role is primarily analytical and report-driven. This position is well suited to individuals who are comfortable working with data, structured documentation requirements and who enjoy producing clear and substantiated reports. Key Responsibilities Business and Operational Assessment • Review clients’ financial statements. • Analyze clients’ business models and operational workflows. • Review employee headcount, manpower composition and organizational structure. • Assess operational scale, space utilization and site requirements. Property Planning Support and Reporting • Prepare structured analyses for industrial property planning. • Draft reports for property-related applications. • Develop structured strategies relating to space requirements and site usage. • Ensure reports and submissions are accurate, consistent and complete. Coordination and Engagement • Obtain information and data from clients. • Clarify data and assumptions with relevant parties. • Respond to queries on reports and supporting materials. • Coordinate documentation with internal and external parties. • Manage multiple assignments and timelines concurrently. Qualifications and Requirements • Ability to review and interpret financial statements. • Strong analytical capability. • Strong written communication and report-writing skills. • Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel. • Organized and detail-oriented. • Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. • Diploma or higher qualification in any discipline. • Prior experience in business analysis, reporting, consultancy support or property-related roles is an advantage. What We Offer • Competitive monthly remuneration. • Performance-based incentives. • Annual performance bonus and Annual Wage Supplement. • On-the-job training and guidance. • Professional and collaborative working environment.

title: IT Service Desk Specialist
description: Position Objective The IT Service Desk Specialist is a highly motivated professional with expertise in technical support, specialising in troubleshooting and analysis. This role is dedicated to delivering superior customer service and ensuring that unresolved issues and inquiries are escalated promptly in accordance with established protocols and Service Level Agreements (SLAs). As an integral member of the IT Service Desk team, the specialist provides Level 0 and Level 1 support for IT incidents and service requests. They act as the primary point of contact for all user incidents, requests, and general communications. Furthermore, the specialist is committed to enhancing user awareness of IT-related issues and promoting the appropriate utilisation of IT services and resources. Responsibilities Key duties and responsibilities are not limited to those listed below: •           Deliver exceptional customer service by promptly and professionally addressing customer needs and concerns. •           Manage IT service incidents reported by school users through the Service Desk Support Portal, specifically those related to personal computers, networks, platforms, and applications. •           Effectively resolve issues or provide alternative solutions to restore IT services to normal operation within the established Service Level Agreement (SLA). •           Escalate unresolved issues or additional support requests to the appropriate support team. •           Provide timely updates to customers regarding the status of outstanding issues in accordance with targeted service levels. •           Ensure accurate and comprehensive documentation of customer interactions and transactions within the IT Service Desk Management System. •           Provide end-user support for classroom technology to educators, assisting with IT and audio-visual equipment such as Interactive TVs, Apple TV, projectors, and associated applications to expedite the return to normal classroom activities. •           Oversee the life cycle management of IT assets, including the registration, tracking, and decommissioning of hardware devices and software licenses. •           Conduct upgrades to PC hardware and software, which includes regular maintenance and installation of service packs, patches, and applications. •           Remain updated on product knowledge, policies, and procedures to furnish customers with accurate and relevant information. •           Execute additional responsibilities as directed by the Head of IT Operations and Business Partnering, the Helpdesk Manager, or other designated personnel within the School. Position Requirements •           Strong analytical and problem-solving skills are essential. The ability to think creatively and identify technical issues for effective resolution is important. •           Practical knowledge and experience in providing IT Service Desk support are required. •           A high standard of customer service skills is necessary. •           Demonstrated ability to work effectively in a team environment, coupled with excellent time management and organizational skills. •           Capable of handling multiple tasks at the same time. •           Proficiency in English, with clear and concise verbal and written communication skills, is important. •           A hands-on approach with the capacity to quickly learn and apply new systems in the work environment is essential. •           Proven ability to provide support for the following: o          Microsoft Windows 10 and later o          Apple iOS o          Apple macOS o          Microsoft Office 365 application suite o          Google Workspace (formerly G Suite) o          IT hardware support for both Apple and Windows devices Qualifications You have: •           A diploma or degree in an IT-related discipline. •           Minimum 1 year of Customer Service Experience in a Technical Service Desk environment preferred. •           Advantage if you have: o          ITIL-related certifications o          Microsoft-related certifications o          Apple-related certifications Working hours: Mon to Fri, 8am to 5pm

title: IT Security Manager
description: You will: 1. Cyber Vigilance: - Monitor and analyze security event logs to identify potential security incidents. - Respond to security incidents in a timely and effective manner, ensuring minimal disruption to business operations. 2. Risk Management: - Conduct risk assessments and vulnerability management to identify potential security threats. - Develop and implement mitigation strategies to minimize risk. 3. Compliance and Governance: - Ensure compliance with IT security policies, regulations, and standard e.g IM8 , PDPA. - Develop and maintain security policies, procedures, and guidelines. 4. Incident Response: - Develop and maintain incident response plans and procedures. - Coordinate incident response efforts with stakeholders, including IT teams and management. 5. Security Awareness and Training: - Promote security awareness and training programs for employees. - Develop and deliver security training sessions to enhance employee knowledge and skills. 6. You Will: First person and POC for all security incidents and escalations The ITSM shall inform the parties listed in the Technical SOP within the Expected Response Timeline of the Incident Management. The information to be provided shall include the incident reference number, description, date and time and the impact (including who had been affected) of the incident. To administer, maintain and assess the implementation of security processes. To develop System Owner support for acquiring and sustaining adequate resources for incident response. To be the corporate intermediary for coordinating communications between System Owner and incident response personnel. To serve as a trusted custodian of incident information for ensuring the preservation and admissibility of evidence. To ensure the compliance with the required IT security policies. To perform the logs review in all security system managed by our operational team To review system security logs and to work with the client manager and onsite technical team on follow up actions. To perform regular policy review and enhancement to ensure all are in line with agency requirements. To promote IT security awareness, latest risk and cyber responsibility. title: Director, Transactions Management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

title: Operations Specialist
description: Headquartered in Singapore, Jurong Port is a leading international multipurpose port operator. Established in 1965, the port initially served Singapore’s Jurong Industrial Estate. Jurong Port has since expanded to serve as Singapore’s main general and bulk cargo gateway. Description for Internal Candidates Headquartered in Singapore, Jurong Port is a leading international multipurpose port operator. Established in 1965, the port initially served Singapore’s Jurong Industrial Estate. Jurong Port has since expanded to serve as Singapore’s main general and bulk cargo gateway. Jurong Port’s multipurpose port operating expertise includes efficient handling of general, bulk and containerized cargo, management and operations of the Tuas Offshore Marine Centre, and Lighter Terminals in Penjuru and Marina South. Besides Singapore, Jurong Port is involved in overseas joint ventures in China and Indonesia. In 2016, Jurong Port’s local and overseas terminals handled close to 35 million tons of general and bulk cargo, and 560,000 TEUs of container cargo. The Job Operational Responsibilities: Monitor port operations and enforce cargo processes to ensure vessel productivity and optimal resource allocation, achieving recommended cargo handling productivity rates. Communicate, control, and troubleshoot all port operations flow and issues within the assigned sector. Liaise with vessel crew regarding safety and operational matters related to the vessel. Conduct checks and facilitate the handover/takeover of the wharf between stevedore companies and relevant departments. Maintain accurate and up-to-date records/documents across multiple platforms pertaining to the overall cargo operations of the vessel. Promptly communicate and update information related to vessel activities, including operational progress and any disruptions or stoppages. Communicate with stevedore vessel checkers and foremen’s gang regarding any matters related to cargo operations. Safety Responsibilities: Oversee cargo handling operations and ensure compliance with safety requirements. Conduct thorough checks on gang workers’ passes and verify the validity of equipment certificates. Review submitted checklists to confirm accurate completion and documentation of necessary safety procedures, equipment checks, and protocols. Review Method Statements, Risk Assessments, and Lifting Plans to ensure alignment with safety requirements. Conduct incident investigations and prepare reports on accidents, near-miss incidents, and safety violations. Maintain accurate records of safety inspections, incidents, and corrective actions taken. Other Responsibilities: Perform other administrative duties and handle ad-hoc tasks as instructed by supervisors. Stay updated on industry best practices, emerging trends, and safety guidelines while proactively incorporating them into daily operations. Job Requirement Minimum ITE educational qualification. Strong knowledge of port operations, cargo handling, and vessel operations would be an added advantage. Candidates with no prior experience are welcome to apply. Proficient in computer skills and basic software applications. Possession of a valid Class 3 Driving License. Possession of relevant safety certificates would be preferrable.WSQ Supervise Safe Lifting Operations WSQ Perform Rigger & Signalman Tasks WSQ Respond to Fire & HazMat Emergency Occupational First Aid Manage/Perform Work at Height Willingness to work in 12-hour shift duties. Ability to work independently, prioritise tasks effectively, and adapt to changing priorities in a dynamic environment.

Coordinate the production of project drawings (e.g. schematics, layouts, installation details, rack elevations etc.)  using Autodesk Revit and AutoCAD, in accordance with company and project standards Lead the QA and QC process as a checker and verifier. Develop complete design packages including detailed drawings, calculations, technical specifications, employer’s requirements, and tender documentation. Be involved with construction site visits to undertake compliance monitoring roles of ICT works, working alongside other disciplines. Collaborate within the wider Cundall team and external partners, working closely with colleagues of various disciplines to support the delivery of technically excellent and successful multi-disciplinary projects. Support the senior management staff within the team, embracing the collaborative and supportive behaviours Cundall exhibit. This includes line management, team mentoring and chartership mentoring. Contribute to commercial planning and management of projects. The Skills Educated to degree level in Electrical Engineering, ICT or Computer Science (MEng, BEng or BSc), and working towards, or already be, a member of a professional institute. You will be aiming for a career in ICT and telecoms systems design and construction in the built environment. We look for 5-8 years of experience working in an engineering design consultancy or for a contractor. Desired traits for the successful candidate include: Ability to take ownership for tasks and deliverables. Capacity to problem-solve and help guide the team and client towards solutions Organised and confident communicator, with the ability to gather client requirements and lead internal and external meetings Knowledge and experience in designing ICT and telecoms systems: Passive ICT systems, such as structured cabling systems and containment. MEP facilities for ICT, including power and cooling. Interface between ICT and other building systems, including BMS and Security. Experienced in leading site visits for surveys, witnessing and commissioning, to support design work. Understanding of project resourcing and commercial management would be advantageous. Working knowledge of drawing and model production using Autodesk AutoCAD and Revit. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English. title: Trustee & Custody Operations Manager
description: About CapBridge CapBridge, a member of FOMO Group, is a leading digital investment platform headquartered in Singapore. As a Capital Markets Services licensee, CapBridge is regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to deal in capital markets products, including securities and collective investment schemes, and to provide custodial services. It is also an exempt financial adviser licensed to issue or promulgate analyses and reports on investment products. CapBridge enables HNWIs and institutional clients to invest in traditional and digital assets via its one-stop digital investment platform, providing highly curated, top-quality, and institutional-grade opportunities to meet clients' diverse asset allocation needs. Through CapBridge's associated company, FOMO Pay Pte Ltd, a regulated MAS Payment Services Act (PSA) Digital Payment Token (DPT) entity, qualified clients can also invest into CapBridge curated products using DPTs, providing a seamless bridge between Traditional Finance (TradFi) and Web3 Finance. About the Role! As the cornerstone of our fiduciary framework, you will spearhead the end-to-end safeguarding of cash, securities, and digital assets. You will provide critical oversight for SPV and trust structures, ensuring every transaction and trustee decision is defensible, compliant, and audit-ready. This role is designed for a seasoned operations leader who can navigate complex MAS regulatory landscapes (SFA, FAA, Trust Companies Act) while driving the evolution of our custody controls and digital asset integration. Key Responsibilities: ● Custody & Asset Safeguarding: Own the end-to-end lifecycle for traditional and digital assets, overseeing reconciliations, corporate actions, and the establishment of robust segregation frameworks. ● Fiduciary Oversight: Lead trustee administration for SPVs and trusts, interpreting complex deeds to monitor issuer obligations, covenants, and formal trustee actions. ● Regulatory & Risk Governance: Act as the first-line owner for MAS compliance (SFA, FAA, TCA); partner with Compliance on inspections and maintain rigorous incident remediation plans. ● Onboarding & AML Oversight: Direct the due diligence and KYC/KYB assessments for issuers and intermediaries, ensuring all counterparties align with internal risk appetite. ● Reporting & Audit Readiness: Serve as the primary contact for auditors and regulators, ensuring all records are inspection-ready and presenting periodic governance reports to senior management. ● Strategic Transformation: Lead workflow automation and UAT for new systems, translating regulatory requirements into scalable operational designs for new asset classes. Requirements: ● Experience: 8–10+ years in Custody Operations, Trustee Administration, or Asset Servicing. ● Regulatory Expertise: Deep knowledge of MAS frameworks (SFA, FAA, Trust Companies Act, and relevant Notices). ● Technical Breadth: Proven experience with SPV structures and capital markets; exposure to Digital Assets or Tokenized Securities is a strong advantage. ● Operational Mindset: Demonstrated ability to act as an independent control owner with a high degree of "audit-ready" documentation discipline. ● Leadership: Experience coaching junior team members and managing complex stakeholder relationships across Legal, Tech, and Senior Management. ● Industry Background: Prior experience in regulated FinTech, Trust Companies, or Fund Administration is highly preferred. Our Perks and Benefits: ● Competitive Compensation and Benefit Package ● Group Hospitalisation and Medical Insurance ● Fun and Dynamic Environment with plenty of learning and growth opportunities ● Career Advancement ● Professional Development Training Workshops ● Team Building Sessions

Lead the identification, evaluation, and acquisition of essential operational resources, including initial equipment, technology platforms, and strategic supplier partnerships, aligned with the company's growth       strategy. Define and champion the company's approach to project execution excellence, stringent quality assurance protocols, and industry-leading safety management systems. Strategic Business Development & Key Client Acquisition: Spearhead business development initiatives by leveraging your extensive network and proactively identifying and engaging with key potential clients, including developers, architects, and other businesses. Contribute strategically to the development of compelling business development materials, presentations, and proposals that align with the company's brand and value proposition. Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with strategic clients, industry influencers, and potential partners to secure initial and future projects. Represent the company at relevant industry events, conferences, and networking forums to build brand presence and generate leads. Provide critical operational and costing expertise in the preparation of complex bids and tenders, ensuring competitive and profitable proposals. Establish robust tracking and reporting mechanisms for business development activities and  outcomes. Project Leadership & Elite Execution (Flagship Projects): Directly lead and oversee the execution of the company's initial, high-profile construction projects, ensuring delivery on time, within stringent budgetary parameters, and to high-quality standards that reflect the company's commitment to excellence. Build, mentor, and empower a team of capable project managers, supervisors, and skilled workers, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary point of operational leadership, reporting directly to the designated General Manager or Business Unit Head on all strategic and tactical matters related to site and project performance. Chair comprehensive project briefings and strategic kick-off meetings, setting clear expectations and ensuring alignment across all stakeholders. Develop and present concise, data-driven periodic progress reports for internal executive review and external client communication. Strategic Construction Planning & Resource Optimization: Provide strategic direction in establishing advanced construction planning methodologies and resource allocation strategies for the new business unit. Establish sophisticated and efficient coordination protocols between internal project teams, pre-qualified subcontractors, key clients, and external consultants, ensuring seamless project delivery aligned with master schedules. Contribute to the development of standardized, company-level cost estimation and control processes, overseeing progress claim reviews, interim certifications, and payment authorizations. Strategically forecast and secure all necessary resources (highly skilled manpower, appropriate equipment fleets, suitable machinery, and quality materials) from project inception to completion, with a strong emphasis on optimized resource utilization and adherence to the company's financial guidelines. Enterprise-Level Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance: Proactively identify and mitigate potential risks across initial projects, translating lessons learned into robust company-wide risk management frameworks and best practices. Ensure unwavering compliance with all Singaporean regulatory requirements, including stringent quality, safety, and environmental standards, establishing a culture of compliance that aligns with the company's ethical and operational standards. Oversee the submission and clearance processes with relevant Singaporean authorities, establishing efficient and compliant protocols for all future projects. Implement and enforce adherence to approved specifications and relevant industry standards,       embedding a commitment to quality assurance throughout the organization. Establish, implement, and continuously monitor a comprehensive safety management system, conducting rigorous periodic safety audits on all initial sites to set the benchmark for a safety-first culture. Strategic HR & Regulatory Framework Development: Leverage a deep understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations to strategically plan and manage foreign worker quotas, work permit applications, and other critical HR practices within the construction sector, aligning with the company's HR policies. Collaborate with the company's HR function to establish the business unit's HR policies, procedures, and talent acquisition strategies, ensuring compliance with local regulations and the company's overall standards. Utilize your comprehensive knowledge of Singapore's business certification and licensing requirements to ensure the new construction firm operates with full legal compliance from its inception, working with relevant internal teams or external advisors. Lead the application and acquisition of all essential business licenses and permits, ensuring a smooth and timely setup process. Strategically plan and manage initial project staffing to optimize workforce deployment while adhering strictly to MOM regulations and quota limitations. Strategic Ad-hoc & Foundational Initiatives: Undertake strategic ad-hoc assignments directed by executive leadership, the Construction Manager, and the Business Unit Head, particularly those critical to establishing the new company's operational, business development, and regulatory frameworks in alignment with the company's overall strategy. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Engineering (Civil, Structural, or equivalent), or a related field from a reputable institution. Advanced degree preferred. Equivalent to 15+ years of progressive and impactful experience within the construction sector in Singapore, with significant and demonstrable success as a Senior Construction Site Manager or equivalent management role on complex, large-scale projects. Exceptional and comprehensive knowledge of best-in-class project management methodologies, tools, and technologies across all project lifecycle stages, with a strategic understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of a new business unit within a growing enterprise. Proven and demonstrable track record of successfully soliciting new business, building strong client relationships, and contributing to significant business development wins within the Singaporean construction market. Deep and current understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations concerning foreign worker quotas, work permit processes, and strategic HR management within the construction industry. Comprehensive knowledge of the necessary business certifications, licenses, and regulatory requirements for operating a construction firm in Singapore, including interactions with relevant government agencies. Mastery of MS Project and the MS Office suite, with proficiency in other relevant project management and collaboration tools. A strategic and passionate leader with exceptional people management skills, demonstrated ability to build and motivate high-performing teams in a start-up or new business unit environment, and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence and innovation. A results-oriented and decisive individual with exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills, crucial for navigating the complexities of a new business and the Singaporean regulatory landscape. World-class written, communication, and interpersonal skills, essential for engaging with executive leadership, diverse stakeholders, building strategic client relationships, securing new business, and effectively interacting with regulatory bodies. Unwavering willingness to work flexibly, including extended hours as required during critical project phases, business development initiatives, and the strategic establishment of the new business unit. A proven ability to thrive under pressure, manage complex priorities, and deliver exceptional results within demanding timelines. A strategic, proactive, and highly entrepreneurial mindset with a strong personal drive to build and shape a significant new construction business unit within a growing enterprise, with a direct and measurable impact on its growth and success across operations, business development, and regulatory compliance. Working Conditions: A dynamic and challenging leadership role based in Singapore, requiring a commitment of 5 working days per week, with the understanding that significant flexibility and extended hours will be necessary, particularly during the      initial strategic phases of the business unit's establishment, critical flagship project milestones, and strategic business development activities. This is a rare and compelling opportunity to leave a lasting legacy by building a significant new construction business unit within a thriving medium-sized enterprise. If you possess the strategic vision, deep industry expertise, entrepreneurial spirit, and proven leadership capabilities to excel in this foundational role, we encourage you to submit your confidential application.

title: Head of IT

title: IT Support
description: You will play a crucial role in supporting the IT Exec and assisting with various IT-related tasks within the organization. This position requires a diligent and meticulous individual who can handle multiple responsibilities, troubleshoot technical issues, manage data entry, liaise with vendors and suppliers, and uphold strict data privacy standards. Responsibilities: Provide support to the IT Executive: Assist in all IT-related tasks, projects, and initiatives within the organization. Troubleshooting and Technical Support: Respond to technical issues reported by employees or outlets. Resolve problems remotely through Zoom or visit outlets for on-site troubleshooting when required. Data Entry and Documentation: Maintain a comprehensive record of all scenarios and cases, ensuring meticulous and accurate data entry for tracking purposes. Time and Task Management: Efficiently manage time and prioritize multiple tasks to meet deadlines and address urgent IT-related issues. Vendor and Third-Party Liaison: Interact with vendors, suppliers, or third parties for obtaining quotes, resolving issues, or coordinating relocations, ensuring effective communication and follow-up. Data Privacy and Confidentiality: Take the protection of company data seriously and strictly adhere to the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA). Obtain necessary approvals before sharing private or confidential information. Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs): Adhere to established SOPs and guidelines to ensure consistency and compliance in IT operations. Continuous Learning: Stay updated with industry trends, technologies, and best practices to enhance knowledge and skills required for the role. Collaboration and Communication: Work collaboratively with team members, colleagues, and stakeholders to ensure smooth IT operations and effective problem resolution. Documentation and Reporting: Prepare reports, maintain documentation, and provide regular updates to the IT Executive regarding ongoing projects, issues, and resolutions. Qualifications and Skills: A diploma or degree in an IT-related field or equivalent work experience is a bonus. Strong technical knowledge and troubleshooting skills in computer hardware, software, and network systems. Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and documentation. Proven ability to manage time efficiently and handle multiple tasks simultaneously. Effective communication skills to interact with vendors, suppliers, employees, and stakeholders. Familiarity with data privacy regulations and commitment to upholding confidentiality standards. Proficient in following SOPs and guidelines to ensure consistent and compliant IT operations. Ability to work independently as well as in a team-oriented environment. Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies and industry trends.

Experience in developing structured user manuals and training; business rules modelling, developing and executing acceptance test plans. Experience in Agile methodology.
