title: Quality Assurance Officer (Facilities Management)
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Quality Assurance & Process Compliance • Review and ensure all FM processes, SOPs, workflows, and checklists comply with ISO standards, client requirements, and industry best practices. • Validate that operational activities align with approved procedures and the QMS framework. • Assist in the preparation, implementation, and improvement of quality assurance policies and guidelines. 2. QMS Support & Continuous Improvement • Participate in QMS reviews, gap analysis, and process enhancement initiatives. • Coordinate change management for document revisions, version control, and procedural updates. 3. Audit & Compliance Monitoring • Assist in planning and conducting internal audits, compliance assessments, and vendor audits. • Track audit findings, NCRs, and corrective/preventive actions (CAPA) to ensure timely closure. • Verify vendor service reports, statutory compliance certificates, and maintenance documentation. 4. Incident & Risk Governance • Support root cause analysis for incidents, faults, and non-conformances. • Track incident trends and recommend preventive measures. • Maintain accurate records for incident reports, near-miss logs, and risk registers. 7. Documentation & Reporting • Maintain QA records, audit logs, compliance matrices, risk logs, and QMS documents. • Prepare weekly/monthly QA reports highlighting issues, recurring gaps, audit results, and corrective actions. • Ensure documentation readiness for client audits, certification audits, and regulatory inspections. Requirements Qualifications • Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, Facilities Management, Quality, or related engineering disciplines. • ISO 9001 Internal Auditor or Lead Auditor certificate is an advantage. Experience • 1 - 3 years of experience in FM, building services, QA/QC, industrial operations, or critical infrastructure. Skills • Strong analytical and documentation skills. • High attention to detail and accuracy. • Able to identify gaps in processes and propose improvements. • Effective communicator with technicians, engineers, vendors, and clients.

title: Middle School Learning Support Teacher
description: Position Responsibilities Middle School Educator Responsibilities Foster a sense of student belonging by cultivating joy and purpose throughout the Middle School. Cultivate a caring and culturally responsive learning community in Advisory, as well as the classroom, taking into account physical, emotional, and intellectual well-being of students. Experience and/or demonstrated skills facilitating restorative conversations when supporting student behavior in the learning environment. Collaborate with subject area teachers to co-identify essential learning and use a planning framework with standards, learning targets, real-world issues, and a culturally responsive curriculum. Co-design quality formative, summative and self-guided assessments that provide students with appropriate scaffolds in order to provide opportunities for frequent feedback and communicate students’ growth and achievement. Plan and implement engaging learning experiences that are inquiry-based, collaborative, culturally responsive, and differentiated. Respond to learners’ diverse needs using responsive, measurable, and research-informed interventions and extensions. Collaborate as a member of a Professional Learning Communities (PLCs) that creates a supportive community, plans curriculum, gives and receives feedback with one another, and engages in professional learning and reflection. Actively engage in professional learning and growth through regular observation and feedback cycles with Professional Learning Community Coaches and Principals. Design and implement inquiry-based learning experiences in and beyond the classroom to prompt learners to question, investigate, create, and reflect. Experience with or interest in co-teaching in a flexible learning environment. Demonstrate an understanding of interdisciplinary teaching and learning. Integrate technology in innovative ways in daily instruction to enhance learning. Engage with students in meaningful activities beyond the classroom that extend student learning experiences (coaching, organizing field trips, sponsorship of clubs, events, overseas travel and/or community service projects, etc.). Strive for continuous self-improvement as a life-long learner. Model the Core Values in your interactions with all members of the school community. Lead and engage with students in meaningful activities beyond the learning spaces, which extend learning experiences for students (supervision, overnight Classroom Without Walls trips, sports coaching, field trips, sponsorship of clubs, events, TRi Time, community service projects, etc.) Connect with and be available to parents and students during and outside of school hours (in person and via email communication) Role-Specific Responsibilities Use diagnostic assessment to determine students’ strengths and needs in literacy, numeracy, and/or executive function. Plan data-informed interventions to support the development of reading, writing, math, and executive function skills. Use a variety of progress monitoring strategies to monitor student progress toward their individualized goals Collaborate in Professional Learning Communities to review data and share progress monitoring documentation and planning for next steps for a plan of intervention. Collaborate with PLC members, students, and parents to write & implement Individual Education Support Plans with SMART goals as well as Individual Accommodation Plans. Collaborate with subject-area teachers and implement a broad range of strategies to support students in a co-taught, inclusive classroom. Participate regularly in professional development to enhance knowledge of best practices for supporting diverse learning needs and delivering targeted interventions in reading, writing, mathematics, and executive functioning. Partner with and assist subject-area teachers in developing and utilizing educational resources to facilitate differentiated student access within the classroom. Collaborates with administrators, counsellors, and specialists to support student well-being and to address social-emotional, executive functioning, behavioral, and academic goals. Position Requirements & Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant field Master’s Degree in relevant field preferred Teaching license or a degree in education At least two years of full-time teaching experience preferred Standards-based curriculum experience preferred Excellent verbal and written English language skills Confident technology skills for teaching and administrative tasks Working Requirements Sponsoring and/or coaching after-school activities are part of the responsibility of the professional educator. Therefore, faculty shall be available to coach and/or sponsor an athletic team or other extracurricular activity. Each faculty member is expected to do a minimum of one Category 1 activity or sport per year. Required attendance at school professional learning days Chaperone and participate in evening and/or weekend school activities each year, as assigned (including “Back-to-School Night”).

title: Director, Business Risk Management
description: Job Summary Strategy •    Support in the latest WRB strategy in pivoting to Affluent market and the execution in Retail Product •    Align to WRB and COO strategic agenda in acquiring client at scale economically through Partnerships. Business •    Work with Group Process Owner of the Product Management Process (For Retail Product Management) / Group Head Partnerships (For Partnerships) and relevant Functional Heads to drive the design, implementation, and maintenance of Product Management related Process / Partnership Governance Framework •    Drive business Operational Risk governance by working with Business Heads and Risk Owners to identify and resolve existing operational risks and horizon risks that is unique to retail products and partnerships. •    Take the lead in identifying and driving initiatives designed to increase business operating efficiency and strengthen first line operational risk management in the Product Management and Partnership process. •    Drive or support transformational change initiatives to align with the latest WRB business strategy in pivoting to Affluent and enable business to grow/scale. Processes •    Work with WRB business members to deliver and maintain optimal global design standards for Product Management Process / Partnership Process that are fit for business purpose and growth. •    This includes end-to-end assessment of operational risk, establishing and maintaining KCIs, CSTs and KRIs to manage operational risk, act on breaches, and approve or remediate deviations from global standards raised by the countries. •    Assess the impact of Group Risk Owner policy changes on the process, amend the relevant governance documents and ensure that the same is cascaded to countries and embedded with proper gap analysis and approved dispensations. Key Responsibilities People and Talent •    Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values.  Set appropriate tone and expectations from their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Ensure the provision of ongoing training and development of people, and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. Risk Management •    Provide business input and oversight of the development of end-to-end scalable infrastructure (where appropriate) and processes to execute business processes pertaining to Product Management and Partnerships.  This includes establishing and maintaining training, advice and support, to assure the processes, policies and standards are effectively implemented in the business. •    Reinforce an effective and exemplary risk and control culture and further strengthen the control environment. •    Act to minimize operational loss and audit failures and take proactive measures to respond to matters arising and identify and manage forward looking risks. •    Overall responsible for the implementation of ERMF/ORF for the process. •    Simplify processes and controls. Governance •    Drive effective governance and oversight of risks arising from Product Management and Partnerships through effective Governance forums and committees, including Retail and SME Banking Product Committee (RS PC) and Partnerships Centre-of-Excellence (Partnerships COE). •    Enhance an integrated governance structure for the robust and efficient governance processes in the business. •    Reinforce an effective and exemplary risk and control culture and further strengthen the control environment. •    Support countries on issues and risk resolutions arising from BAU activities, audits, risk reviews etc within the processes. •    Responsible to ensure that key stakeholders and governance forums are kept up to date on key issues and resolution plans and overall progress on initiatives. Regulatory and Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key stakeholders •    WRBMT and business (mainly Group Head of CCPL, Group Head Partnerships and respective Group Heads of Products) •    WRB COO Top Team •    Key functional Heads o    Head, CFCC WRB o    Head of Legal WRB o    Head, OTCR, WRB o    Head, ESGR, WRB •    Country WRB Heads, and respective partnerships / retail product business heads Other Responsibilities •    Embed Here for good and Group’s brand and values in WRB COO team •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. Our Ideal Candidate •	10+ years relevant risk management experience and/or business experience in Wealth and Retail Banking. •	Demonstratable prior experience in risk identification and control design will be a key advantage. •	Knowledge of the Banks Enterprise Risk Management Framework, Policy and Standards is a plus. •	Familiarity with Product Management and Partnership Business. •	Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. •	Strong interpersonal, relationship building and stakeholder management skills. •	Ability to think strategically as well as be able to execute. •	Collaborative and creative problem solver, while being outcome focused. •	Ability to make decisions in an unstructured environment and to work under pressure to meet tight deadlines. •	Ability to multitask and work independently in a fast-paced environment Role Specific Technical Competencies •	Knowledge of Non-Financial Risk Management (ERMF, Risk and Control Self-Assessment) •	Knowledge of Retail Business and products, including Partnership business. •	Knowledge of Product Management •	Experience of running any Committees or Forums would be a plus About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. title: Electrical Engineer
description: Job Summary: The Electrical Project Engineer will play a crucial role in the planning, design, and execution of electrical and ELV systems for industrial/commercial construction projects in Singapore. This includes overseeing the installation, commissioning, and maintenance of electrical systems, ensuring they meet both safety standards and regulatory requirements. The ideal candidate will have experience working on construction projects, collaborating with contractors, architects, and other engineers to deliver high-quality electrical solutions on time and within budget. Key Responsibilities: 1. Electrical Design and Planning: Develop detailed electrical design plans for the construction project, including power distribution, lighting systems, electrical control systems, and ELV systems. Collaborate with architects, mechanical engineers, and other specialists to integrate electrical systems seamlessly into the overall building design. Conduct site surveys and coordinate with other teams to collect necessary data for design. 2. Project Management: Assist in creating and maintaining the project schedule for electrical works, ensuring key milestones are met and deadlines adhered to. Support the Project Manager in managing project budgets, estimating electrical materials, labor, and other resources required. To perform material take-off to prepare BQ (Bill of Quantities). Ensure that all electrical installations are carried out as per approved designs, on time, and within the allocated budget. Coordinate procurement of electrical equipment, ensuring compliance with project specifications and timelines. 3. Installation, Testing, and Commissioning: Supervise and coordinate the electrical installation, ensuring all work is performed in accordance with drawings and specifications. Oversee the testing and commissioning of electrical systems to ensure proper functionality and compliance with safety standards. Provide on-site troubleshooting and problem-solving during installation and testing phases. 4. Quality Control and Compliance: Ensure all electrical installations are compliant with local regulations, such as the Singapore Standards (SS), Singapore Electrical Code, and relevant building codes. Conduct inspections during and after installation to verify that electrical systems meet quality standards. Prepare required documentation for compliance checks, including test reports and as-built drawings. 5. Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) Management: Ensure that all electrical work complies with safety regulations and the  company’s health, safety, and environmental policies. Conduct site safety inspections, ensuring that electrical installations are carried out safely and risk assessments are performed. 6. Team Coordination and Supervision: Support Site Supervisor to supervise electrical teams, subcontractors, and other personnel involved in electrical installations and tasks on-site. Support Project Manager in meetings with project teams, subcontractors, and stakeholders to track project progress and address any issues. 7. Client and Stakeholder Liaison: Maintain regular communication with clients, contractors, and consultants to update them on the progress of electrical works and address any concerns. Provide technical support and recommendations to clients regarding electrical design, system efficiency, and maintenance requirements. Manage any client requests for changes or modifications to electrical systems during the project lifecycle. 8. Documentation and Reporting: Ensure that all project documentation is accurately maintained, including electrical schematics, reports, and inspection logs. Provide regular progress reports to the Project Manager, highlighting any challenges, delays, or variations in scope. Key Requirements: 1. Education : Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. 2. Experience : At least 3-5 years of experience in electrical engineering within the Singapore construction industry. Experience in managing electrical systems for large-scale construction projects, such as commercial or industrial developments. 3. Technical Skills : Proficient in electrical design software such as AutoCAD. Proficiency in Revit is a bonus. Strong knowledge of electrical systems, including power distribution, lighting, emergency systems, and ELV (emergency voice communication, CCTV, Card Access) systems. Familiarity with relevant Singaporean electrical standards and building codes (e.g.,SS 638, SS 546, SS531 etc.). 4. Project Management : Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, with a keen eye for detail and quality control. Understanding of project schedules, cost management, and resource planning. Experience in coordinating subcontractors and vendors. 5. Skills : Strong organizational and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for interacting with clients, teams, and stakeholders. Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines. 6. Language : Proficiency in English is required Preferred Qualifications: Previous experience with industrial / commercial construction projects in Singapore. Familiarity with Building Information Modeling (BIM) for integrated design and collaboration.

description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Since 1985, GreenGarden Integrated Services Pte. Ltd. has been providing exceptional cleaning and landscaping services across Singapore. From commercial cleaning to landscaping maintenance and building upkeep, we take pride in delivering quality, reliability, and outstanding customer service. At GreenGarden, we believe in investing in our people—equipping our staff with the skills, knowledge, and mindset to grow and succeed. Join us and be part of a team that values professionalism, teamwork, and continuous learning. We are proud to be recognized as a BizSAFE Enterprise Level STAR, holders of the Clean Mark Silver Logo, and ISO certified, reflecting our strong commitment to safety, quality, and operational excellence. Take the next step in your career with a company that truly cares—join GreenGarden Integrated Services today! Job Summary The Management Trainee (Corporate Services) programme grooms future corporate professionals through an 18-month structured rotation, delivering hands-on experience in financial accounting, procurement, budgeting, compliance, and digitalisation to prepare trainees for permanent roles within the organisation. Responsibilities Support procurement and finance teams by preparing, organising, and maintaining documentation to ensure accurate tracking and timely reporting of financial transactions and procurement activities Assist in compiling and reviewing compliance and audit materials to facilitate smooth audit processes and adherence to corporate governance standards Participate in process improvement initiatives by identifying inefficiencies and contributing to the design and implementation of streamlined, digitalised workflows Collaborate with cross-functional teams to coordinate corporate services operations that align with organisational objectives and enhance operational effectiveness Contribute to cost optimisation reviews and digitalisation projects by analysing data and recommending actionable improvements to support strategic initiatives Engage in departmental attachments and rotations to develop a comprehensive understanding of various corporate functions and build cross-disciplinary expertise Preferred competencies and qualifications Diploma or Degree in Business Administration or a related discipline Previous internship or project experience in procurement, finance, or corporate services Proficient in Microsoft Excel and other digital tools to support data analysis and reporting Strong analytical and problem-solving skills demonstrated through practical application in academic or internship projects Effective written and verbal communication skills applied in professional or academic settings

title: Senior/ Facilities Executive (Commercial Grade A Building)
description:
Responsibilities Carry out regular site checks to ensure the building and facilities are well-maintained, safe, and fully  operational. Manage day-to-day building operations including security services, cleaning, landscaping, waste handling, irrigation systems, car park operations, and rectification of defects. Regulate and oversee the proper use of shared facilities and common areas to ensure compliance with house rules. Coordinate with term contractors to ensure all servicing and maintenance works are completed according to agreed schedules and service standards. Conduct routine inspections of the premises, including car parks, to ensure cleanliness, signage visibility, and overall upkeep. Support budget planning and monitoring for operating expenses and capital works related to building management. Perform regular checks on fire protection systems and organise periodic fire drills to ensure safety readiness and regulatory compliance. Engage and communicate effectively with tenants, members of the public, contractors, and authorities on building matters, improvement works, emergencies, and compliance requirements, including after office hours when necessary. Requirements: Diploma in Building Management, Building Services, or a related discipline. Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage a wide range of stakeholders. Service-oriented mindset with a proactive and positive approach to work. Able to work independently while also collaborating effectively as part of a team. title: Service Contract Specialist
description:
Job Overview The Service Contract Specialist, part of the Service Back-Office team, is responsible for managing all aspects of service contracts. The role’s primary objective is to ensure timely proposal and renewal of contracts, maintain
profitability, and accurately manage contracts within the system. Job Responsibilities and Essential Duties Daily responsibilities Review and prepare data analysis and findings on service contracts profitability for review meetings using Power-BI. Extract and prepare service contracts profitability summary card for management review. Prepare proposals for tender submissions to customer’s platform. Send contract proposals to customers promptly. Follow up with customers and revise contract proposals as needed. Review Master Service Agreement contract terms and conditions from customer to safeguard the company’s interests. Ensure contracts comply with company guidelines. Collaborate with the Senior Service Contract Specialist to prepare contract proposals, de-escalation disputes and contractual issues. Establish pricing structures for service contracts based on product lines and contract levels. Month-End responsibilities To prepare monthly service slides on service contracts. Generate monthly service contract KPI reports (e.g, renewal rate, profitability, trends). Data Maintenance Create warranty and service contracts in SAP and archive the hard copies. Monitor the expiration of existing warranty and service contracts. Update contracts in SAP when equipment is decommissioned, ensuring documentation is current. Administrative responsibilities To be an SAP Key User and a subject matter expert (SME) for service contracts in the service team. Provide support to the Service Coordination Team if required. Perform any ad-hoc duties assigned by the line manager and/or the Company. Minimum Requirements Proven experience as a service coordinator and/or customer service role with 3-5 years working experience Minimum Diploma holder in any discipline. Required Knowledge, Skills and Abilities Possess excellent communication and organizational skills. Able to work in a fast-paced environment. Able to work independently and as a team. Proficiency in MS Office and SAP is preferred.

title: Business Development Executive (Logistic / Freight)
description: Key Responsibilities Business Development and Client Acquisition Identify and develop business opportunities in the logistics and supply chain sector, with a focus on growing cross-border export services. Build and maintain a robust sales pipeline through market research, networking, and lead generation. Possess experience in one of the following key areas: Singapore & Southeast Asia Focus: Proven experience in managing export logistics flows within the Southeast Asia corridor (e.g., Singapore-Malaysia, Singapore-Indonesia). Global Markets Focus: In-depth knowledge of logistics protocols and requirements for key international trade lanes such as Asia-Pacific (e.g., Japan-Korea, ANZ) or Europe-America, with a proven ability to optimize client supply chains for cost and efficiency. Client Relationship Management Proactively maintain and strengthen relationships with existing clients through regular follow-ups and highly responsive support, ensuring their ongoing satisfaction. Point of contact between the company and the client, deeply understanding their challenges in cross-border exports to deliver comprehensive, end-to-end logistics solutions. Design competitive service proposals tailored to client needs for routes to Southeast Asia, Japan-Korea, Europe-America, and Australia-New Zealand. Sales Strategy and Planning Develop and implement effective sales strategies to achieve and exceed sales targets. Design differentiated sales approaches for various regional markets (e.g., Southeast Asia, Europe-America) to enhance the market penetration of the company's logistics services. Market Analysis and Insight Closely monitor international logistics industry trends and competitor activities to identify potential opportunities and risks in the cross-border export sector. Provide regular market feedback to the management team to support strategic decision-making for key routes (e.g., Singapore-Malaysia, Japan-Korea, Europe-America). Sales Reporting and Management Prepare and submit regular sales reports, including sales forecasts, client feedback, and market trend analysis. Utilize CRM tools proficiently to efficiently manage customer interactions and the sales process. Negotiation and Contract Management Negotiate terms and conditions with clients to secure profitable and sustainable business partnerships. Ensure the accuracy and timeliness of all relevant documentation (e.g., transport agreements, customs clearance documents).

title: Assistant Contract Manager (Tender focus)
description: Key Responsibilities: Assist in the preparation and compilation of tender submissions including technical proposals, pricing, and contractual terms. Analyse tender documents (e.g. drawings, specifications, conditions of contract) and identify risks, clarifications, and requirements. Coordinate with internal departments (QS, engineering, procurement) and external stakeholders (consultants, suppliers, subcontractors) to gather tender inputs. Ensure accurate and timely submission of tenders in accordance with client requirements. Maintain and update the tender tracking system, and support pre- and post-tender clarifications. Assist in cost estimation, BOQ review, and value engineering proposals. Support the Contract Manager in negotiating terms and conditions for awarded contracts. Ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in tender and contract processes. Requirements: Degree in Quantity Surveying, Building, Construction Management, Engineering, or related discipline. 5-8 years of experience in tendering or contracts administration in the construction/engineering sector. Strong understanding of contract types and tender processes. Proficient in MS Office and contract management software. Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills. Able to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines. Experience in school and hotel project tenders. Familiarity with local tender platforms and procurement systems. Knowledge of legal and financial aspects of contracting.

title: Freelance Private Tutor (Primary and Secondary)
description: Job Description: Private Tutor (Primary and Secondary Levels) Company Overview: We are Singapore’s first dedicated matching platform for children with learning or special needs. Our mission is to connect children with freelance professionals across five key areas: Tuition, Early Education, Early Intervention, Therapy & Counseling, and Enrichment Activities. We strive to create a supportive community that bridges students' needs with expert guidance, helping them grow and thrive academically and emotionally. Position: Private Tutor (Primary and Secondary Levels) Responsibilities: Provide one-on-one tuition to primary and secondary school students. Develop personalised lesson plans and resources that align with each student’s learning needs and school curriculum. Utilise creative and engaging teaching methods to support students in achieving academic success, particularly those with learning or special needs. Assess students’ progress and provide timely feedback to parents and guardians. Work closely with parents, caregivers, and our administrative team to ensure consistent support for the student. Stay updated on the latest teaching methods and special education trends, especially for students with SEN (Special Educational Needs). Requirements: Minimum of a diploma in education or relevant subjects. MOE or Special Education qualifications are highly regarded. Prior experience working with children, especially those with special needs or learning difficulties, is preferred. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work independently and customise learning plans based on the student's progress and needs. Patience, empathy, and a passion for helping students achieve their potential. Why Join Us? Enjoy Higher Rates : Our rates are adjusted for inflation and aligned with the latest market standards, ensuring you are fairly compensated. No More Sudden Cancellations : We collect deposits before the first session, so you don’t have to worry about last-minute cancellations. No Wasted Trips : If a client doesn’t show up, you will still receive compensation for the first session. One-Time Administration Fee : Any additional sessions or direct referrals won’t incur further administrative fees from Special Minds. Boost Your Profile : Gain visibility through the free promotion of your profile on our social media platforms, helping you connect with more clients. Meaningful Career : With the growing demand for special needs education (SEN) in Singapore, this is your opportunity to gain valuable experience and build a meaningful career helping students with unique needs. Additional Income Opportunity : Take on assignments that fit your schedule and preferences to earn extra income in your free time, or choose full-time opportunities for more consistent earnings! Compensation: Part-Time Tutor : Up to $60/hr Full-Time Tutor : Up to $90/hr Early Childhood Educator : Up to $70/hr MOE/Special Ed Tutor : Up to $140/hr Enrichment Specialist (dance, art, swimming, etc.): Up to $120-150 per session Therapist (speech, occupational, physiotherapy, etc.):Up to $200-220/hr Psychologist : Up to $250/hr Counsellor : Up to $150/hr If you’re passionate about education and want to make a difference in the lives of students with learning or special needs, join us at Special Minds. Together, we can create a brighter future for every child. APPLY HERE: https://portal.specialminds.com.sg/?type=service_professional For more information, please visit us at: https://specialminds.com.sg/ If you have further queries, please email us at hello@specialminds.com.sg , or contact us at 8721 8722.

title: Quantity Surveyor (A&A, Additions & Alterations)

The role requires strong cost control, stakeholder coordination, and hands-on exposure to live A&A construction environments. Pre-Contract / Tender Stage Prepare cost estimates, budgets, and feasibility studies for A&A works Take-off quantities and prepare Bills of Quantities (BQ) Assist in tender documentation, evaluation, and contract award Review drawings and specifications to identify cost implications and risks Post-Contract / Construction Stage Administer contracts in accordance with SIA / REDAS / PSSCOC forms Prepare and assess progress claims, variation orders (VOs), and final accounts Monitor project costs, cash flow, and budget compliance Evaluate subcontractor claims and manage payments Handle claims, cost reports, and value engineering proposals A&A-Specific Scope Manage cost implications for phased works, night works, live operational sites, and tight timelines Coordinate with consultants, site teams, subcontractors, and clients on design changes Assess cost impact of unforeseen site conditions common in A&A projects Ensure compliance with BCA regulations, authority submissions, and contract requirements Stakeholder & Reporting Attend site meetings and coordinate closely with Project Managers and Site Teams Prepare regular cost reports and financial forecasts for management Support audits and internal cost controls Requirements Diploma / Degree in Quantity Surveying, Building, or Construction Management Minimum 2 years of relevant QS experience in A&A projects Strong knowledge of SIA / PSSCOC contract conditions Experience working with main contractors or A&A specialists Experience in commercial, residential, retail, or institutional A&A works Ability to manage multiple small-to-medium A&A projects concurrently How to Apply : Click Apply Now — or send your resume directly to Talent@BuildBridgePartners.com EA Details: EA Name: BuildBridge Partners Pte Ltd EA License No: 25C2918 EA Reg No: R1551092 – Khoo Yan Jie

title: Hotel Front Office Receptionist
description: Position summary statement: The incumbent will check in/out guests according to hotel procedures and ensure all guests’ accounts are correct and settled upon check out. Attend to all guests’ enquiries and complaints and to ensure guests’ satisfaction. Primary Responsibilities: Sell, register and assign rooms to incoming guests, ensuring that registration cards are filled up correctly. Check out departing guests as per procedures. Inform relevant departments of arrival, room changes, check out and special arrangements. Accepts reservation in the absence of Reservation Assistant. Record in Handover List of any special arrangements and important matters for follow-up. Liaise with Duty Manager or Front Office Supervisor regarding any complaints. Perform cashiering and maintain a complete record of guests’ account. Check all guests’ bills before presentation to guest upon check out. Charge all monies due to the hotel to the respective travel agents or companies. Declare all shortages or excess to Accounts Department. Attend to all requests and requirements from guests promptly and handle complaints tactfully. To ensure all guests preferences are met to the best of our ability and strive to (MADDAM) “Make A Difference, Do A Little More”. Create a positive first impression to guests with regards to corporate image. Read entries in Communication Book and ensure all instructions and tasks assigned are follow-up. Ensure procedures and policies on city ledger are followed. To be aware of all VIP and Special Attention guests’ arrivals and departures. Ensure all Pan Pacific Discovery Members, VIP and Special Attention guests are met up by Duty Manager, and benefits are explained upon check in. To undertake any other reasonable assignment by the Duty Manager and Front Office Manager, as and when required.

title: Senior Manager, Learning & Development
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 1000 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES The Senior Manager, Learning & Development is responsible for establishing the organisation's learning and development strategy and recommending organisational design and/or change interventions to deliver business strategies and plans effectively. He/she collate and monitor the budgets for learning and development programmes to build capabilities in the organisation. He/she influences stakeholders to redesign the organisational model and spearheads organisational change interventions to drive the integration of Competencies / Skills Frameworks across the organisation's learning programmes. He/she develops and oversees talent development programme to support succession planning, career progression, and employee engagement. He/she builds leadership capability across all levels through Integrating leadership frameworks (e.g. Clifton Strengths, Serving Leader) into development pathways. Job Responsibilities Conduct learning needs analysis to assess their current capabilities and areas of development required to deliver against the organisation's strategy, business restructuring and future plans. Develop customised learning and development strategies, plans and curricula to improve overall workforce capabilities for current and future roles, and to promote lifelong learning. Engage with senior management / HODs to seek clarity on business strategy and develop organisational learning and development strategies based on organisation's current and future business and skill requirements. Compile and monitor budget for learning and development and prioritise spending to invest on strategic organisational capabilities to enable current and future business strategy and objectives. Monitor and assess the effectiveness and business impact of learning and development strategy and programmes to meet current and future business needs. Apply data governance concepts and principles to identify, collect and prepare data for analytics and HR metrics benchmarking. Look across a series of data and anticipate implications of business activity on HR practices. Review and develop HR policies, practices or strategies that competitively position organisation as an employer of choice. Develop communication material/ information kits for the HR programmes/ activities to convey desired learning and development messages clearly and effectively. Create high impact, consistent and accurate messaging using appropriate tools to drive staff learning and development, aligned with the organisation-wide communications strategy. Understand organisational and individual challenges, help the senior leaders to define the problem and identify root cause of the development related issues Leverage Skills Maps of the Skills Frameworks to identify learning gaps and develop competency framework. Create and align learning roadmaps and programmes with reference to MINDS Career Maps and MINDS Competency Framework. Establish Learning & Development strategy in accordance with MINDS Competency Framework components. QUALIFICATIONS Degree in Human Resource Management or Business Administration or equivalent HR Professional Certification OTHER INFORMATION At least 10 years of relevant working experience, with at least 5 years in a middle to senior L&D role. Strong leadership and supervisory skills, with exemplary personal conduct and professionalism. Excellent communication, negotiation and interpersonal skills both written and verbal. Coaching, System thinking, Leadership Development background Should preferably have experience in HR transformation projects leading to excellence in organisation culture or learning & development or HR practices. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

This role requires strong technical accounting knowledge, high integrity, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.
